automation_critic_detail: With the Critic on, Shepherd watches every pull request an agent opens. The moment its CI checks all pass (turn green), a separate, read-only review agent starts up in an isolated copy of the repo. It reads the pull request's full diff, hunts for bugs, security problems and anything that doesn't actually satisfy the task, and writes back a verdict: either "looks good" or a list of concrete findings, each tied to a file and line. The review never edits your code; it only reports. A diff that was merely rebased and force-pushed is recognised as identical and is not reviewed twice.
automation_autoaddress_detail:
Auto-Address closes the loop after the Critic: whenever the Critic reports findings, they go straight back to the agent that wrote the pull request — it fixes them and pushes a correction. You don't relay anything by hand.

CI then runs again and the Critic re-reviews the new version. This repeats until no findings remain — capped at 3 rounds by default (adjustable in Settings).

If the agent declined a finding on the PR with a reason, the Critic sees that note in the next round and won't raise the same objection blindly again.

Only works while the Critic itself is on.
automation_plan_gate_detail: The Plan gate inserts a checkpoint before any code is written. A gated agent is told to first lay out its plan in a file instead of coding. Once the plan is ready, a separate reviewer agent reads it, pulls in context from the codebase (read-only) and actively tries to poke holes in it, surfacing risky assumptions, missing steps or a wrong approach. Its objections go back to the planning agent, which revises the plan; this repeats until the plan holds up, up to a configurable round limit (five by default, adjustable in Settings). Only then is the agent released to implement. If you're watching live you can also approve early or demand changes yourself. While the gate is active during planning, Autopilot is paused, and it resumes once the plan is approved.
automation_learnings_detail: Learnings lets the team get smarter over time instead of repeating the same mistakes. In the background Shepherd watches sessions for signals (agents getting blocked, stalling or hitting errors) and periodically distills them into proposed "house rules": short pieces of standing guidance, each with a reason and the evidence it came from. Nothing is applied automatically: you review the proposals and promote the ones worth keeping. Every rule you promote is then prepended to the system prompt of every new agent in that repo, so they all start with the lessons already learned. There's a size budget, and the most recently-relevant rules are kept first when space runs short.
automation_autopilot_detail: Autopilot keeps a single agent moving when it would otherwise stop and wait for you. Every time the agent pauses (at a menu, a yes/no question, a stall or "waiting for input") a small classifier reads the terminal to work out why it stopped, then nudges it onward with the right answer: resume, fix a red CI, or open a pull request once the work is done. It only handles these routine stops; anything that genuinely needs a human is escalated to you instead. A step limit (10 by default) stops a runaway loop, and Autopilot normally hands back once a PR is open. It's off during the Plan gate's planning phase and switches itself back on afterwards.
automation_autodrain_detail:
Auto-Drain works through your issue backlog on its own: Shepherd watches your tracker and automatically spawns an agent for the next issue carrying the chosen label whenever capacity is free. You no longer start each issue by hand.

The dials below set the limits: how many auto-agents may run at once (Limit), which label to pick up (Label), and the account-usage ceiling (%) above which no new agents are started.

Each started issue is tagged so two Shepherds never grab the same one. When an agent's PR is ready, it waits for a human to merge (or goes to Full-auto merge) — freeing the slot for the next issue.
automation_automerge_detail: Full-auto merge lands finished pull requests for you, end to end. It only acts on fully-automatic sessions (ones where both Autopilot and this switch are on) and watches each of their PRs. When a PR is genuinely ready (open, CI green, no conflicts, up to date with the main branch, and, if the Critic is on, with a clean verdict on the current version), the Merge train merges it. If a PR has fallen behind the main branch, it first steers the agent to rebase and re-checks everything before merging. Persistent conflicts are capped (5 rebase attempts) so it pauses for you instead of thrashing. Nothing is merged until every one of these checks passes.
automation_buildqueue_detail: The Build queue turns a big task into a reviewable plan before the agent charges ahead. Right after reading the task, the agent writes out an ordered list of steps, which appear here as cards you can edit, reorder or remove. Nothing runs until the plan is approved (you click Approve, or, if Autopilot is on, it's approved for you) after which the agent works through the steps in order, marking each done as it goes, all within one continuous session so no context is lost. The agent can also revise the remaining steps mid-run if it learns something that changes the plan, while completed steps stay put. It's independent of Autopilot and the Plan gate and can be combined with either.

herd_merge_train_prompt:
Run a merge train for the pull requests I've flagged ready to merge. Review each for correctness and product-fit, propose a safe merge order, and stop for my approval before merging anything; then re-check the queue after every merge, because main moves under you.

Ready-to-merge PRs:
{prs}

If this repo provides a /merge-train command or skill, use it. Otherwise work the train yourself:
1. For each PR, gather status with gh (CI rollup, mergeability/conflicts, how far behind main, and which files overlap other PRs in this list).
2. Move any PR that is CI-red, conflicting, gate-failing, or that we don't actually want into a separate hold list with a one-line reason; never merge past a red gate.
3. Order the rest: smallest and most independent first; when two overlap, sequence them so the riskier one rebases onto the other; release-please PRs last.
4. Present the numbered merge order plus the hold list and WAIT for my approval.
5. On approval, merge in order: rebase onto latest main first (never merge main into the branch), confirm gates are green, then gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch, and re-check the remaining PRs against main before the next one.

feat_per_role_effort_title: Set reasoning effort per helper role
feat_per_role_effort_body: Settings now has an Effort column beside each helper role's model. The critic ships dialed up and the cheap/mechanical roles (recap, namer, doc agent) dialed down; the planner inherits your session's effort by default. Change any of them per role. The variant/compare picker gained a matching Effort control too, so parallel runs can differ on effort, not just model.
feat_critic_effort_guardrail_body: Settings now warns when you lower the Critic role's reasoning effort below high. The critic is a rigor role, so a reduced effort weakens PR review — the warning nudges you to keep it dialed up.

gloss_reasoning_effort_def: A cost/quality dial (low, medium, high, xhigh, max) that sets how much the model reasons before answering. Higher effort spends more tokens for deeper reasoning; lower is faster and cheaper. Shepherd passes it to the agent CLI as --effort (Claude) or model_reasoning_effort (Codex).

settings_about_blurb: Shepherd is self-hosted mission control for interactive Claude Code: genuine terminal sessions you observe and steer by typing, on your own server and subscription. It is equally opinionated about how agent-built software ships: the Plan gate and the Critic review every plan and PR adversarially, approved Learnings become house rules, and the Merge train lands nothing that is red or behind its base.

halt_all_aria: Halt the herd: interrupt all {count} running agents
halt_arm_aria: Confirm halt; activate again to interrupt all {count} running agents
halt_failed: Halt failed: no agents interrupted. Retry.
session_autopilot_paused_title: Autopilot handed this back for a decision: {question}
session_autopilot_complete_title: Autopilot finished: task complete, nothing to open as a PR: {summary}
session_autopilot_toggle_failed: Couldn't change autopilot for this session. Try again.
readiness_load_error: Couldn't load the readiness scorecard. Try again.
readiness_not_applicable_body: The readiness baseline covers JavaScript / TypeScript and Rust repositories. Support for other stacks is on the way.
readiness_all_covered: Every baseline guardrail is in place.
readiness_claudemd_present_note: This repo already ships agent instructions; the task will merge, not overwrite.
readiness_adopt_intro_create: Make this repository AI-ready: install the guardrails below and add a CLAUDE.md with these house rules. Open a PR; don't commit to the default branch.
readiness_adopt_intro_merge: Make this repository AI-ready: install the guardrails below and merge these house rules into the existing CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md without overwriting it. Open a PR; don't commit to the default branch.
readiness_g_pre_push_ci_removes: Without it the agent pushes red and waits on a human CI round-trip to learn what broke.
readiness_g_git_hooks_removes: Without a hook manager, none of these checks run automatically before a push.
readiness_g_type_checker_removes: Without it, type errors surface in your review instead of failing the agent first.
readiness_g_linter_removes: Without it, you flag lint nits by hand that the gate could catch.
readiness_g_formatter_removes: Without it, you hand-fix style in review that the formatter would settle.
readiness_g_test_runner_removes: Without tests in the hook, regressions reach you instead of failing the agent first.
readiness_g_agent_instructions_removes: Without house rules, you re-explain the same posture in chat every task.
readiness_g_ci_removes: Without CI, nothing independently re-checks what the agent self-reported.
readiness_g_lint_staged_removes: Without it, the whole tree is re-checked or skipped; staged-only keeps the hook fast.
readiness_g_commit_lint_removes: Without it, you correct commit-message format by hand.
readiness_g_dead_code_audit_removes: Without it, dead exports and unused deps accrete and you spot them by eye.
readiness_adopt_gitignore_note: Opens a PR adding Shepherd's session artifacts to .gitignore for repos you can push to; otherwise they're already hidden locally.
readiness_adopt_gitignore_no_access: No push access; session artifacts are already hidden locally via git exclude.
readiness_adopt_gitignore_no_forge: No git forge configured; session artifacts are already hidden locally via git exclude.
feat_adopt_gitignore_body: The Readiness tab can now open a PR adding Shepherd's session artifacts (.shepherd-*) to a repo's .gitignore in one click, for repos you can push to. Where you can't, they stay hidden locally via git exclude, so they never clutter your diffs either way.
feat_pr_kind_badges_title: Code vs. bot PRs in Repos
feat_pr_kind_badges_body: The repo list in Repos now shows your real code-PR count, with separate badges for Dependabot and release-please PRs so automated PRs don't look like pending work. The PRs tab tags each PR by kind too.
feat_relaunch_body: Right-click (or long-press) any card and choose Relaunch to spawn a fresh task with the same prompt and current settings (model, repo, base branch and plan-gate included) then decommission the original. The replacement starts fresh: with plan-gate on it re-plans, and a task that came from the drain becomes an attended task awaiting your Approve.
feat_title_rename_body: Double-tap (or double-click) the session name in the header to rename it right there — no need to hunt for the little ✎ button. A single tap still shows the model profile and token usage.
feat_relaunch_repo_body: Relaunch a task in another repository — the New Task dialog opens pre-filled with the task's prompt, model and base branch so you can pick a different repo to land it in.
feat_readiness_body: The Repos page has a new Readiness tab: it scores a repo's guardrails against Shepherd's dogfooded baseline, prescribes the tooling that removes the most human↔AI back-and-forth, and generates a tailored CLAUDE.md you can send straight to a task.
draft_reconcile_promote_error: Draft promote failed for {desig}; check the PR and retry
draft_reconcile_enforce_error: Draft enforce failed for {desig}; check the PR and retry
feat_automerge_body: Turn on Full-auto merge to let Shepherd land ready PRs for you: it rebases onto main and re-verifies before every merge, so parallel tasks stay safe.
buildqueue_empty: No steps yet. The agent will author a queue when it starts.
buildqueue_awaiting_hint: The agent paused here. Review the steps, then Approve & run to let it start.
feat_buildqueue_body: Enable the Build queue flag on a repo to let the agent author an ordered step list before it runs. You curate the steps (edit, reorder, delete, add) then click Approve & run. Under Autopilot the queue is auto-approved.
feat_plan_gate_body: Turn on the Plan gate to align before the night starts: the agent grills you and writes a plan, then a second Claude adversarially reviews it until it holds up; only an approved plan runs. Enable it per task in New Task or per repo in the automation panel.
feat_backlog_repo_filter_body: Narrow the repo list in Repos to repos with open issues and/or open PRs using the new toggle chips.
feat_newtask_repo_first_title: Repo now leads the New Task form
feat_newtask_repo_first_body: The repository you'll launch into now sits at the top of the New Task form and is named on the Create & Run button, so you never start a task against the wrong repo by accident.
prspanel_merge_train_prompt:
Run a merge train for the pull requests I've selected. Review each for correctness and product-fit, propose a safe merge order, and stop for my approval before merging anything; then re-check the queue after every merge, because main moves under you.

Selected PRs:
{prs}

If this repo provides a /merge-train command or skill, use it. Otherwise work the train yourself:
1. For each PR, gather status with gh (CI rollup, mergeability/conflicts, how far behind main, and which files overlap other PRs in this list).
2. Move any PR that is CI-red, conflicting, gate-failing, or that we don't actually want into a separate hold list with a one-line reason; never merge past a red gate.
3. Order the rest: smallest and most independent first; when two overlap, sequence them so the riskier one rebases onto the other; release-please PRs last.
4. Present the numbered merge order plus the hold list and WAIT for my approval.
5. On approval, merge in order: rebase onto latest main first (never merge main into the branch), confirm gates are green, then gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch, and re-check the remaining PRs against main before the next one.
prsfilter_drafts_desc: Hide pull requests still marked as draft
prsfilter_conflicts_desc: Hide pull requests with merge conflicts
prsfilter_failing_ci_desc: Hide pull requests whose CI checks failed
prspanel_none_match: no PRs match the current filters
feat_pr_filters_body: Hide draft, conflicting, or CI-failing PRs and narrow to one author in a repo's PRs tab. The hide toggles persist across repos and reloads.
feat_backlog_pr_merge_train_title: Launch a merge train from selected PRs
feat_backlog_pr_merge_train_body: Multi-select open PRs in a repo's PRs tab in Repos and launch a merge train scoped to just those PRs.
feat_header_fold_title: Fold the header for more terminal
feat_header_fold_body: On mobile, tap the chevron in the session header to collapse the tabs and PR rail and hand that space to the terminal. Your choice sticks across sessions.
feat_learnings_per_repo_title: Learnings show on the repo chip
feat_learnings_per_repo_body: A repo's pending house rules show as a ✦ on its chip in the herd's repo filter. Filter to that repo and click the ✦ to open the learnings drawer.
feat_update_checkout_body: When you merge a PR from one of the app's merge buttons, a toast offers to fast-forward that repo's local default branch to the freshly merged state; no manual pull needed.
feat_automation_help_body: Every switch in the automation panel now has a clickable "ⓘ" that opens a thorough, plain-language explanation of what that function does, how it works, and exactly when it fires, so anyone, not just the team, can understand the Critic, Plan gate, Autopilot, Auto-Drain and the rest before turning them on.
feat_critic_activity_title: See what the Critic is doing live
feat_critic_activity_body: The Critic runs as its own Claude Code session. Hover the REVIEWING badge to see its latest action live (“$ git diff”, “read review.ts”) instead of just that it's busy.
feat_preview_body: When an agent's dev server is running, a Preview badge appears on its row. Opening it shows the live app in an in-HUD pane, reachable from desktop or phone over Tailscale.
feat_repo_roles_body: Set who reviews and who merges a repo in its automation panel. When the merger is someone else (e.g. a teammate who lands PRs), a green PR reads "Waiting on them" instead of "Your turn".
feat_backlog_active_highlight_title: See at a glance which issues are already claimed
feat_backlog_active_highlight_body: Issues a Shepherd agent has claimed carry a “shepherd:active” label. In the issue list in Repos it now stands out in amber, so you can tell taken work from free work at a glance instead of reading every chip.
feat_repo_filter_body: Click a repo in the herd's repo filter to show only its agents; click it again to show every repo.
feat_resume_session_body: When an agent exits and leaves a session sitting at a bare shell (e.g. after a herdr restart), it no longer strands you. A ↻ Resume button now appears in the session header (and right-clicking, or long-pressing, any session card offers Resume too) to respawn the provider's resume command and drop you straight back into the conversation.
feat_draft_mode_body: Turn on Draft mode to open every new PR as a draft; it stays out of the merge queue until signed off. Choose who may promote it: a human, the Critic, or either. Mutually exclusive with Full-auto merge.
feat_preview_start_title: Start a dev server from the HUD
feat_preview_start_body: When an agent has no live preview yet, a Start control appears on its Viewport. Click it to have the agent launch its dev server (auto-detected from package.json, or type the command yourself) then preview the running app without waiting for the agent to start one on its own.
feat_active_label_newtask_title: Claimed issues stand out in New Task
feat_active_label_newtask_body: When you seed a New Task from a GitHub issue, any issue already claimed by an active Shepherd agent now shows its shepherd:active label highlighted (surfaced first and tinted with the running-session hue) so you can tell at a glance which issues are already being worked on.
feat_issue_search_body: The Issues tab in the Repos view now has a search field: type to narrow the list by number, title, body, or label instead of scrolling through dozens of issues.
feat_recent_repos_title: Recent repos pinned to the top of the picker
feat_recent_repos_body: The repo picker in New Task now pins the three repos you've run the most agents on over the last 3 days to the top, set apart and ranked by agent count, so the repos you're actively working on are one click away instead of buried in the alphabetical list.
feat_fable_body: Anthropic's most powerful model is now selectable in New Task. Pick "fable" for the hardest, longest-horizon work. Heads-up: it's the premium tier with higher token costs than Opus, so reach for it when the task is worth it.
feat_fable_weekly_title: Fable's weekly limit is now shown
feat_fable_weekly_body: Claude Code gives Fable its own separate weekly limit. The usage panel now surfaces it as its own bar — in the top-bar popover and the Usage dashboard's Limits tab — so you can see how close you are to the Fable-specific cap, not just the aggregate weekly window.
feat_usage_provider_sections_title: Usage is now labelled per provider
feat_usage_provider_sections_body: The usage popover and mobile sheet now split into clearly-labelled sections — "Claude Code usage" for the 5-hour/weekly/Fable limits and credits, and "Codex usage" for the token counts — so it's obvious which rows belong to which coding CLI.
feat_tally_filter_body: The Busy / Idle / Blocked counts in the top bar are now buttons: click one to show only sessions in that state, in the session list and the grid view. Click it again, hit Herd, or use the chip in the list header to clear the filter.
fable_arrival_body: Anthropic's most powerful model is now available in Shepherd. Pick "fable" in New Task for the most demanding work. It's the premium tier (higher token costs than Opus) so save it for the tasks that earn it.
feat_preview_tailscale_body: Live previews now register themselves over Tailscale automatically as each agent's dev server comes up, and unregister when it stops, so only in-use ports are exposed. No more one-time tailscale serve port setup.
feat_whatsnew_versiondate_title: Each update is now dated and versioned
feat_whatsnew_versiondate_body: Every entry in this What's-New drawer now carries the release version it shipped in and the date that release went out, so you can see at a glance how recent each change is and which version introduced it.
feat_backlog_recent_repos_body: The repo list in Repos now starts with the three repos you've recently run the most agents on, ranked by the same criteria as the New Task repo picker's "recently worked on" group.
feat_product_story_body: Shepherd now says what it is: opinionated mission control for interactive Claude Code. The About section in Settings carries the two-pillar story (genuine sessions you steer by typing, and firm opinions on how agent-built software ships) and the README leads with it.
feat_herd_keynav_body: Reach any session without the mouse: j/k (or the arrow keys) cycle through the herd in rail order, 1–9 jump straight to the Nth session, and g hops to the next session that needs you. The keys stay out of the way while you type in the terminal or a form.
feat_preview_stop_title: Stop a preview to reclaim RAM
feat_preview_stop_body: Force-stop a previewed dev server from the preview pane to free its memory. Operators can also enable opt-in idle-stop (SHEPHERD_PREVIEW_IDLE_STOP_MS) to auto-stop idle, unwatched previews.
feat_card_repo_icon_title: Repo emoji replaces the repo line on cards
feat_card_repo_icon_body: When a repo has a configured emoji, session cards now show it right before the session name instead of spending a whole line on the repo name: denser cards, same at-a-glance repo identity. Hover the emoji to see the repo name; click it to show only that repo's sessions, click again to clear the filter. Repos without an emoji keep the familiar repo line.
unitrow_repo_filter_clear_aria: Clear the {repo} filter, show all sessions
feat_viewport_repo_icon_title: Repo emoji replaces the repo name in the phone terminal header
feat_viewport_repo_icon_body: On small screens, when a repo has a configured emoji, the terminal header now shows just the emoji instead of emoji plus repo name, freeing that width for the session title. Tap the emoji to show the repo name again; tap once more to hide it. Repos without an emoji keep showing their name as before.
feat_slim_header_body: The bar above the terminal is more compact: the dev-server start button is now icon-only, autopilot and status show as tight pills and a status glyph. GitHub Issues have moved out of a per-session Issues tab and now live in the Repos pane instead.
settings_default_model_hint: Sets the Claude Code model for new tasks and for Claude-backed autonomous drain/autopilot spawns. Auto keeps autonomous spawns on Claude's own default; the launch-window default is the Fable promo if active, otherwise Claude's own default. Choosing an explicit model makes all unattended Claude Code spawns run that tier.
settings_default_model_premium_warning: This model tier is premium. All autonomous drain and autopilot spawns will run at this cost unattended.
settings_default_model_1m_note: The 1M context window processes far more tokens per turn, raising the cost of every unattended spawn. Sonnet's 1M tier draws from usage credits.
feat_model_1m_body: Pick "Opus (1M context)" or "Sonnet (1M context)" from any model picker — New Task, the global default, or a repo's automation settings — to run sessions with the 1-million-token context window. It's a premium tier: the larger window means higher per-turn token cost, so choose it for genuinely large-context work.
feat_default_model_body: Set a global default model for every new task and all autonomous drain/autopilot spawns from Settings → Coding CLI. "Auto" keeps the launch-window default; "default" locks in Claude's own model; or pick a specific alias to always run that tier.
feat_herd_keynav_anywhere_title: Session switching from inside the terminal
feat_herd_keynav_anywhere_body: Session switching now works while you're typing in the terminal: Alt (⌥ on Mac) + j/k, g, or 1–9 switches without leaving the keyboard; Shepherd captures these combos, so those key combinations never reach the agent's terminal. With the terminal unfocused, plain j/k/g/1–9 now chain freely (switching no longer pulls focus back), and Enter returns focus to the terminal.
feat_session_switch_tab_title: Switch sessions with Alt+Tab or Alt+[ ]
feat_session_switch_tab_body: Alt+Tab (or Alt+]) jumps to the next session and Alt+Shift+Tab (or Alt+[) to the previous one, cycling the herd in rail order and wrapping at the ends — the same navigation as Alt+J/K, and working even while the terminal holds focus. If your window manager reserves Alt+Tab for its own switcher, use the bracket keys — they always reach Shepherd.
feat_stepper_legend_title: Task bar shows each pipeline stage
feat_stepper_legend_body: Each session card's progress bar now tracks five stages (Planning, Implementing, PR, Review, and Ready) with the PR segment tinted by CI status and the Review segment by the review verdict. Hover the bar on desktop to see a stage legend.
feat_desktop_decom_body: Hover a session row to reveal a ✕, or use the decommission control now always visible in the session header: two clicks (arm, confirm) decommission any session, PR or not. Right-click a row for the full menu.
feat_usage_limit_push_body: Shepherd now sends a push notification when the 5-hour Claude usage window crosses 80%, once per window, so you can wind down or queue work before hitting the cap.
issuespanel_action_aria: Start a session running “{label}” on this issue; skips the New Task dialog
steerseditor_hint: Each entry can show as a chip in the session steer bar and/or as a quick-launch button on backlog issues. An emoji keeps it recognizable when space is tight.
steerseditor_scope_bar_title: Show as a chip in the session steer bar
steerseditor_scope_issues_title: Show as a quick-launch button on backlog issues
steerseditor_repos_title: Limit this steer to specific repos
feat_issue_actions_body: Define multiple prompts that launch straight on a backlog issue; the old single standard command became the first one. Steers and issue actions now share one editor in Settings, and each entry can carry an emoji so buttons stay recognizable when space runs out.
steerseditor_scope_none_error: every entry must surface somewhere: enable “bar” and/or “issues” on the highlighted row
feat_steer_labels_body: On the cramped mobile steer bar, tap the right-anchored “ABC” key to show every button's text label instead of just its emoji, so you can tell the steers apart when you forget what a glyph means. Tap again to collapse back to icons. The choice is remembered per device.
feat_usage_gauge_detail_body: Hover the 5H / WK usage bars in the top bar and a detailed card now opens (each window with its full name, a wide bar, the exact percentage and when it resets) instead of the old one-line tooltip.
timetip_waiting_merge_burning: {who}! The merge button is gathering dust! {ago} 🔥
feat_time_tooltip_body: Hover a session card to see what its clock is made of: runtime, last activity, and how long an open PR has been waiting on review or merge, with escalating emoji the longer it sits 🔥.
newproject_name_invalid: Use only lowercase letters, digits, dots, hyphens, and underscores; must start with a letter or digit
newproject_idea_placeholder: Describe what you want to build…
newproject_warning_github: Project created locally; the GitHub step failed, publish it from the terminal.
newproject_failed_slug: Invalid project name: use lowercase letters, digits, dots, hyphens, and underscores only
newproject_failed_exists: A folder with that name already exists
newproject_failed_outside: Target is outside the projects folder
newproject_failed_identity: Set your git identity first: git config --global user.name "Your Name" and git config --global user.email "you@example.com"
newproject_failed_gh_auth: Not logged in to GitHub; run gh auth login.
newproject_failed_gh_exists: A GitHub repo with that name already exists.
newproject_failed_remote: Could not create the GitHub repository.
newproject_failed_git: git failed while bootstrapping the project.
newproject_prd_seed_noidea: (no description was provided; infer a reasonable starting scope and ask clarifying questions in the PRD)
feat_new_project_body: Create a fresh git repo (optionally on GitHub) and kick off the first agent run, all without leaving Shepherd.
feat_sidebar_collapse_body: On touch tablets and unfolded foldables, collapse the Herd sidebar to give the terminal more room. Your choice is remembered.
feat_herdr_release_notes_link_body: The herdr update panel now links to the full upstream release history on GitHub, handy when you're jumping across several versions and want every version's notes, not just the latest.
feat_repo_switcher_title: The repo filter is now a chip rail
feat_repo_switcher_body: The repo filter is now a row of chips above the herd. Click a repo to scope every view to it; click it again to show all repos. A repo's pending learnings and pauses show on its chip, with the rest of its drain detail when you filter to it. Filtering now works in the grid view too.
feat_redraw_menu_body: Terminal history squished after viewing a session from a narrower device? The new ↔ Redraw button in the session header offers four repair variants to try, from a gentle resize nudge to a full provider resume re-render.
feat_backlog_repo_search_title: Search the repo list in Repos
feat_backlog_repo_search_body: Type in the new filter input at the top of the repo list in Repos to narrow repos by name; combines with the Has issues / Has PRs chips.
feat_steerbar_edit_body: When the steer bar isn't crowded, a pencil button sits at its right edge — tap it to jump straight into the steers editor.
automation_sandbox_profile_hint: Sandboxing is opt-in. Standard and Autonomous confine the agent to its worktree and block ~/.ssh, ~/.aws, sibling repos, and other $HOME dotfiles. Autonomous also restricts outbound traffic to an allowlist (Anthropic + the forge host); other egress is blocked. Standard confines the filesystem but leaves network open — suitable for interactive-only sessions. The gh/claude auth tokens stay readable but cannot be exfiltrated to arbitrary hosts under Autonomous.
automation_sandbox_profile_caveats: Sandboxed repos must use an HTTPS git remote (SSH-remote repos can't push), and MCP servers whose dependencies live outside the bound paths may fail to load.
session_sandbox_degraded_title: Requested sandbox unavailable — this agent is running unconfined.
session_sandbox_autonomous_title: Filesystem-confined to the worktree AND network-egress restricted to an allowlist (Anthropic + the forge host); other outbound is blocked. Auth tokens stay readable but can't be exfiltrated to arbitrary hosts.
session_sandbox_standard_title: Filesystem/process-confined to this worktree; blocks ~/.ssh, ~/.aws, and sibling repos. Network stays open by design (interactive-only). The gh/claude auth tokens stay readable but are exfiltratable.
session_sandbox_unconfined_title: Unattended agent running unconfined — no sandbox applied.
feat_sandbox_body: Each repo can now pick a sandbox profile in its automation panel: Trusted (no sandbox, today's behavior), Standard, or Autonomous. Standard and Autonomous confine the agent to its worktree. Autonomous is required for auto/drain sessions and additionally confines network egress to an allowlist (Anthropic + the forge host), blocking all other outbound. Sessions surface their applied sandbox state as a badge.
newtask_sandbox_hint: Per-task sandbox override. Leave at 'Repo default' to inherit this repo's configured profile. standard/autonomous confine the agent to its worktree and block ~/.ssh, ~/.aws, and other $HOME dotfiles. Autonomous also adds the egress allowlist (Anthropic + the forge host), blocking other outbound; Standard leaves network open (interactive-only). The gh/claude auth tokens stay readable.
epic_warn_no_deps: No dependencies declared — all {count} children will run in parallel. Add blocked-by links or an `epic-dag` block to enforce a build order.
epic_hold_blocked: Held — {desig} needs you; new starts paused.
epic_hold_changes: Held — {desig} has changes requested; new starts paused.
epic_hold_error: Held — {desig} hit a review error; new starts paused.
epic_hold_cap: At capacity ({inFlight}/{max}) — new starts paused until one finishes.
epic_hold_empty: Nothing eligible to start right now.
epic_hold_disabled: Drain is off for this repo.
feat_epic_runner_title: Work an ordered epic of issues
feat_epic_runner_body: Point Shepherd at a tracking issue and it works the child issues in dependency order — one [[pr|PR]] per issue, advancing as blockers merge.
feat_epic_provider_handoff_title: Choose the coding CLI for epic children
feat_epic_provider_handoff_body: Epic runs can now pick Claude Code or Codex for future child spawns, and an existing child can be continued in-place with another CLI without losing its Shepherd session, worktree, issue, or PR mapping.
feat_native_sub_issues_body: Issues with native GitHub sub-issues (no markdown checklist) now show a SUB-ISSUES badge in Repos, expandable just like epics.
feat_session_epic_badge_body: A session working a tracking issue now shows an EPIC badge with live sub-issue progress — click it to open that epic in Repos.
epic_badge_open_title: Epic #{number}: {merged}/{total} sub-issues done — open in Repos
epic_badge_open_aria: Open epic #{number} in Repos ({merged} of {total} sub-issues done)
readiness_g_dependency_automation_remove: Without it, dependency bumps pile up as manual busywork instead of automated PRs you just review and merge.
feat_dependency_automation_body: The Repos → Readiness tab now scores whether a repo keeps dependencies current with Dependabot or Renovate, and prescribes it when missing.
feat_pr_target_branch_title: PR rows show a non-default target branch
feat_pr_target_branch_body: In the Repos → PRs tab, a PR that targets a non-default (epic/stacked) branch now shows a small chip with its target, so stacked PRs no longer look identical to ones targeting the default branch.
feat_epic_grouping_body: Sessions spawned from an [[epic|epic]]'s sub-issues now gather under a collapsible EPIC headline at the top of the list, showing live progress, attention cues, and a shortcut to the epic in Repos.
automation_allprs_desc: Critic reviews every open CI-green PR, not just agent ones
automation_allprs_detail: Normally the Critic only reviews pull requests opened by Shepherd's own agents. Turn this on and the Critic instead sweeps the whole repo on a timer, reviewing every open, CI-green pull request — including ones humans or other tools opened, and fork contributions. Each gets the same read-only diff review, posted as a comment (never a blocking change request, so it won't gate third-party PRs). This is independent of the per-session Critic switch and reviews more PRs, so it costs more tokens. Each PR is reviewed once per pushed head; a pure rebase isn't re-reviewed.
automation_smelllens_desc: Give the Critic a named code-smell checklist — flagged as non-blocking notes
automation_smelllens_detail: An experimental, opt-in trial (default off). It appends a named vocabulary of code smells — Fowler's Refactoring catalogue, trimmed to nine high-signal ones (mysterious names, duplication, feature envy, data clumps, primitive obsession, repeated switches, shotgun surgery, divergent change, speculative generality) — to the session Critic's prompt, turning its vague "quality problems" line into a concrete checklist. Every match is a judgement call and lands as a non-blocking note in the review body — never a finding, never a blocking change request, so it can't burn auto-address rounds. It adds tokens to each review round, so it ships off by default while we measure whether the extra signal is worth the cost. Only works while the Critic itself is on.
feat_critic_all_prs_body: Enable "Review all PRs" in a repo's automation panel and the [[critic|Critic]] will review every [[ci|CI]]-green pull request — not just ones tied to a Shepherd session. Opt-in per repo; note the higher token cost for busy repos.
feat_critic_smell_lens_title: Trial: a code-smell lens for the Critic
feat_critic_smell_lens_body: An experiment we're trialing: enable "Code-smell lens" in a repo's automation panel to hand the [[critic|Critic]] a named checklist of nine code smells (duplication, feature envy, primitive obsession, and more). Matches surface as non-blocking notes in the review body — never blocking findings. It's off by default and costs extra tokens per review round, so we're shipping it opt-in while we measure whether the added signal earns its keep.
feat_host_capacity_title: Diagnostics now checks host resource guardrails
feat_host_capacity_body: Settings → Diagnostics gained a Host capacity check. It warns when a systemd-managed host has no MemoryMax/CPUQuota limits, and errors when the host is under dangerous memory/IO pressure — so you can spot a risky deployment before it thrashes under many sessions. On non-systemd or local dev hosts it stays quiet.
feat_steers_prompt_editor_title: Roomier steer editor with slash commands
feat_steers_prompt_editor_body: In Saved Steers, clicking into a prompt field now expands it to show the whole text, and typing "/" offers the same slash-command picker as the New Task and compose fields — pick a command with the arrow keys or a tap.
automation_default_model_hint: Overrides the global default model (Settings → Session) for this repo. Inherit defers to the global setting; an explicit choice preselects the New Task picker and drives autonomous drain/autopilot spawns in this repo.
feat_repo_default_model_body: Set a default model per repository in its automation panel — it overrides the global default and preselects the New Task picker. Leave it on Inherit to keep following the global setting.
topbar_credits_stale: snapshot stale — refresh for live spend
settings_extra_credits_ceiling_hint: Pauses autonomous drain and autopilot once pay-as-you-go extra-credit spend (real money beyond your subscription) accrued since the weekly window reset exceeds this amount, in your account currency. 0 pauses on any new extra-credit spend. Overage carried over from an earlier week stops counting once the weekly window resets, so the pause tracks your subscription cadence — not the monthly credit reset.
settings_extra_credits_ceiling_save_fail: Couldn't change the extra-credit spend ceiling. Retry.
feat_extra_credits_body: When you opt into paid pay-as-you-go usage, the top bar now shows a CR gauge with money spent against your monthly cap — and flags it the moment real spend starts. Open it to see the amount, reset, and snapshot age, or refresh on demand.
session_sandbox_egress_degraded_title: Autonomous filesystem sandbox active, but the network-egress backend is unavailable, so outbound is unrestricted — tokens are exfiltratable. Don't over-trust this run.
toast_uploads_dropped: {count} attached file(s) expired and could not be restored. The session started without them
toast_injection_detected: Heads up: issue content for a session tripped {count} prompt-injection check(s). It is fenced as untrusted data — review before trusting the result.
toast_untrusted_author: Auto-drain skipped issue #{issue}: its author is not a trusted repo member, so Shepherd will not start it unattended. Start it manually if you trust it.
toast_sessions_stranded: {count} session(s) lost their agent after a herdr restart. Revive to bring them back.
toast_revive_all_failed: Couldn't revive the stranded sessions. Retry.
toast_auto_revived: Auto-revived {revived} stranded session(s); {failed} couldn't be revived.
stranded_revive_title: Bring the agent back (restores the full conversation)
herd_revive_all_title: Bring back every session whose agent died after a herdr restart
feat_egress_body: Autonomous agents are now confined to an allowlist of hosts (Anthropic + the forge, plus operator extras); all other outbound connections are blocked. Blocked attempts surface as inline alerts. When the backend is unavailable, a visible warning badge appears so you know the session is running with unrestricted network.
settings_contrast_hint: Boosts every text and border pair to WCAG AAA — for readability in bright sunlight and outdoors. Applies on top of the dark or light theme above.
feat_mobile_contrast_body: Settings → Device now carries the high-contrast toggle alongside the light/dark switch, so you can crank readability to WCAG AAA right on your phone — built for steering sessions outdoors in bright sunlight.
diagnostics_hint_herdr_outdated: Your herdr version is below the supported floor — update herdr.
diagnostics_hint_herdr_missing: herdr binary not found in PATH — install it to run agent sessions.
diagnostics_hint_herdr_offline: herdr is installed but its server is offline — agent sessions can't run. Start it by running `herdr server`, then re-run Diagnose.
diagnostics_hint_herdr_unsupported: This herdr version is newer than Shepherd supports — agent spawning would fail on it. Use Downgrade herdr to return to the highest supported version in one click, or pin herdr manually (https://herdr.dev), then re-run Diagnose.
diagnostics_hint_tailscale_not_serving: Tailscale is running but isn't fronting Shepherd — set up tailscale serve or a Tailscale Service for remote access.
diagnostics_hint_tailscale_missing: Tailscale not installed or not logged in — install it and run tailscale up.
diagnostics_hint_git_missing: git not found in PATH — install it before running tasks.
diagnostics_hint_gh_not_authenticated: GitHub CLI missing or not logged in — run gh auth login.
diagnostics_hint_gh_unverified: Couldn't verify GitHub CLI auth — the check timed out. Shepherd will re-check shortly.
diagnostics_hint_claude_missing: Claude Code CLI not found in PATH. Install Claude Code or Codex to run agent sessions.
diagnostics_hint_claude_trust_ok: Claude Code trusts this repo folder — the usage gauge can read it.
diagnostics_hint_claude_trust_untrusted: Claude Code hasn't trusted this repo folder, so the usage gauge may stay blank. Mark it trusted to fix.
diagnostics_hint_bun_outdated: Your bun version is below the supported floor — run bun upgrade.
diagnostics_hint_bun_missing: bun not found in PATH — install it from bun.sh.
diagnostics_hint_node_outdated: Your node version is below the supported floor — update node.
diagnostics_hint_node_missing: node not found in PATH — install it before running tasks.
diagnostics_hint_host_capacity_unbounded: Shepherd runs as a systemd service with no memory or CPU ceiling, so a burst of sessions could starve the host. Add a [[host-capacity|host capacity]] limit — How to fix has a copy-paste command.
diagnostics_hint_host_capacity_pressure: The host is under memory or IO pressure right now — pause or reduce active agent sessions until it recovers. How to fix covers adding limits so it can't recur.
diagnostics_hint_host_capacity_uninspect: Shepherd can't verify host resource guardrails on this deployment.
diagnostics_hint_herdr_health_ok: No leftover herdr panes — every live pane maps to an active session.
diagnostics_hint_herdr_health_leftover: Some herdr panes are still running processes with no active Shepherd session behind them — likely leftovers from finished tasks. Nothing was touched (diagnose-only). Note: systemd's Tasks count for herdr reflects [[herdr-hygiene|threads, not processes]], so a high number there isn't itself a leak.
diagnostics_hint_herdr_health_uninspecta: Shepherd can't reach herdr to check runtime hygiene right now.
plugins_intro: Install and manage server-side plugins. Plugins run in-process with full server privileges; expand a loaded plugin to see what it publishes.
plugins_install_hint: Paste a public GitHub repo URL, then Activate it — no restart needed. Cloned as-is — a plugin with its own dependencies needs a manual install (and a restart) before it loads.
plugins_trust_warning: Plugins run in-process with full server privileges and are not sandboxed. Only install code you wrote or fully trust.
plugins_confirm_uninstall_body: Remove {name}? This deletes its folder. A running plugin keeps working until you restart Shepherd.
plugins_activate_needs_restart: "{name}" loaded with an error — it may need its dependencies installed (bun install), then a restart of Shepherd.
plugins_restart_banner: Restart Shepherd to apply your changes.
plugins_err_url_credentials: Remove the credentials from the URL.
plugins_err_url_invalid: That doesn't look like a GitHub repository URL.
plugins_err_folder_exists: A folder with that repository name already exists.
plugins_err_manifest: That repository has no valid plugin.json.
plugins_err_disabled: That plugin is disabled in its plugin.json.
plugins_err_api_version: That plugin targets a different plugin API version.
plugins_err_id_taken: A plugin with that id is already installed.
plugins_err_clone_auth: Repository not found, or it requires credentials.
feat_plugin_install_body: Settings → Plugins now installs a plugin from a GitHub URL — paste the repo, confirm, then Activate it (no restart needed). It also lists every installed plugin and lets you uninstall.
feat_plugin_activate_body: A freshly installed plugin now has an Activate button in Settings → Plugins — it loads in-process, so its routes, hooks and gear/UI go live immediately. No restart needed for the common case (a plugin shipping its own dependencies still needs bun install + a restart).
feat_plugin_repo_link_body: Installed plugins with repository metadata now show a REPO link in Settings → Plugins, beside Update, Activate and Uninstall, so you can inspect the source without plugin-authored UI.
feat_server_plugins_body: Run private, out-of-repo extensions on your instance. A new Settings → Plugins panel shows each loaded plugin's health and published status.
diagnostics_subtitle: Prerequisites Shepherd needs to run smoothly.
onboarding_intro: We checked your environment — here's what Shepherd needs to work.
onboarding_pick_root_intro: Shepherd only looks for repositories inside this folder. You can change it later in Settings.
feat_diagnostics_body: Shepherd now checks its prerequisites — herdr, tailscale, git, gh, claude/codex, bun, and node — and warns you in Settings → Diagnostics when something's missing or could be configured better.
diagnostics_hint_gh_missing: GitHub CLI (gh) not found in PATH — install it, then run gh auth login.
diagnostics_hint_pwa_ios: On iOS, Shepherd's push notifications only work when it's installed to the Home Screen — in a Safari tab you get none. Tap the Share button, then 'Add to Home Screen'.
diagnostics_hint_pwa_android: Install Shepherd for an app icon, a full-screen view, and a push subscription that survives the browser's storage cleanup. Open the browser menu → 'Install app' / 'Add to Home Screen'.
diagnostics_hint_pwa_optional: Installing Shepherd is optional in this browser. Where it's supported, it adds an app icon, a full-screen view, and push notifications that survive the browser's storage cleanup.
feat_pwa_install_body: Diagnostics now shows whether Shepherd is installed to your Home Screen. On iPhone/iPad, push notifications only work when installed — the new row tells you when you're missing out and how to fix it.
mergetrain_confirm_other_repos: {count} ready PR(s) in other repos are not included.
feat_session_recap_body: Shepherd now generates a concise recap after each session — verdict, headline, and open items — so you can see what happened at a glance.
newtask_research_hint: Web research → a report PR or a GitHub issue. No code changes. Plan gate and autopilot don't apply and are disabled.
newtask_research_sandbox_note: Research can't use the autonomous profile — web search needs open network access.
research_badge_title: Research task: web research → a report PR or a GitHub issue
feat_research_body: Start a Research task from New Task: an attended agent does web research and delivers a report PR or opens a GitHub issue. Research never opens a code PR.
integrated_epics_chip_hint: Merged by Shepherd into the integration branch: {merged} of {total} sub-issues
integrated_epics_child_closed_hint: Issue closed without Shepherd merging a PR
integrated_epics_footer_opening: All sub-issues done — Shepherd is preparing the landing PR
integrated_epics_footer_nothing_merged: All {total} sub-issues were closed without a Shepherd merge — nothing to land · #{number} still open
integrated_epics_footer_nothing_to_land: Nothing left to land · #{number} still open
integrated_epics_dismiss_failed: Couldn't dismiss the epic — try again
feat_integrated_epics_body: When an epic's sub-issues all land, it no longer vanishes — a new 'Integrated epics' band at the bottom of the Herd keeps it, with each child's PR and a one-click dismiss once you've followed up.
settings_colorblind_hint: Shows a shape next to the session emoji on phones (✓ waiting, ! blocked) so those states don't rely on the green/red header tint alone. Off by default — turn it on if green and red are hard to tell apart.
feat_colorblind_markers_body: If green and red are hard to tell apart, switch on Colourblind status markers in Settings → Device. A ✓ (waiting) or ! (blocked) then sits next to the session emoji on phones, so those states no longer rely on the header tint alone. Off by default.
feat_quick_theme_title: Quick theme & contrast in the menu
feat_quick_theme_body: On phones, tap the gear (top-right) to switch dark/light theme and toggle high contrast right from the menu — no more digging into Settings → Device. The menu now sits flush to the edge with a blurred backdrop so it reads as the focus.
integrated_epics_landing_failed: Couldn't open the landing PR — retrying
feat_epic_landing_pr_title: Epics land via a final PR
feat_epic_landing_pr_body: When an epic's children are all integrated, Shepherd opens a final PR that lands the whole epic and closes the parent issue — surfaced as a link in the Integrated-epics band.
recap_unavailable: No recap record exists for this session; the reason was not recorded.
recap_predates_feature: This session finished before session recaps were available, so none was recorded.
herd_done_title: Show finished (archived) sessions and their recaps
feat_done_lens_body: Filter the Herd to your finished and merged sessions, then open any one to review its read-only recap of what happened.
settings_auth_mode_hint: How Claude Code sessions authenticate. API-key mode bills metered API rates under the Commercial Terms instead of your subscription.
settings_auth_key_missing_warning: API-key mode is selected but no key is set — agent spawns are paused until you add one.
usage_subscription_only: Usage tracking is subscription-only — see the Anthropic Console.
feat_api_key_auth_body: Opt into billing spawned agents against an Anthropic API key (Commercial Terms) instead of your subscription — Settings → Coding CLI.
feat_tappable_slash_commands_body: Tap a slash command Claude suggests in the terminal (e.g. /squad, /gsd-quick) to drop it into the prompt — no retyping on mobile. It's inserted, not sent, so you review and press Enter yourself.
settings_auth_key_verify_timeout: Timed out waiting for a response.
feat_api_key_verify_body: Check that your saved Anthropic API key actually authenticates a spawned agent — right from Settings → Coding CLI, before it fails on first use.
feat_readiness_install_commands_body: The Readiness prescription now lists the exact install commands for your repo's package manager, so each missing guardrail can be adopted without looking the steps up.
feat_readiness_rust_body: The Repos → Readiness tab now analyzes Rust/Cargo crates, not just JavaScript/TypeScript. It scores clippy, rustfmt, cargo test, the pre-push CI mirror and Cargo Dependabot, and generates a Rust-tailored CLAUDE.md with the exact rustup/cargo setup commands.
feat_docs_link_title: Documentation link in the top bar
feat_docs_link_body: A link to the Shepherd documentation now sits in the top bar next to the gear, and inside the gear menu — so the docs are one click away from anywhere.
feat_mobile_menu_docs_title: Docs & version in the mobile menu
feat_mobile_menu_docs_body: On phones the gear menu now links straight to Shepherd's home — the README and docs — and shows the build version you're running.
epic_migrations_pending: {count} migration file(s) — verify before merge (not run in-harness)
integrated_epics_ack_migrations_failed: Couldn't acknowledge the migrations — try again
feat_epic_migrations_body: When an epic's landing PR adds database migrations, the Integrated-epics band now shows a warning chip with the file count — the harness never runs them, so verify them before merging. Clearing the row asks you to acknowledge the migrations first.
feat_newproject_owner_title: Choose the owner for a new GitHub repo
feat_newproject_owner_body: When you create a project with a GitHub repository, you can now pick whether it lands under your personal account or one of your organizations — the dialog lists every org you belong to instead of always defaulting to your account.
forkrepo_hint: Forks the repo under your GitHub account and clones it. Pull requests you open from it target the original (upstream) repo. The fork stays in your account.
forkrepo_failed_auth: Not logged in to GitHub (or no permission to fork). Run gh auth login.
forkrepo_failed_url: Invalid repository — use owner/repo or a GitHub URL
forkrepo_failed_outside: Target is outside the projects folder
feat_fork_repo_title: Fork a repo to contribute upstream
feat_fork_repo_body: The repo picker now has a “Fork a GitHub repo” option next to Clone. Paste owner/repo (or a URL) and Shepherd forks it under your account, clones it, and wires the remotes so pull requests you open target the original (upstream) repo — the way to contribute to a project you don't have write access to.
feat_backlog_repo_link_title: Repo name links to its forge
feat_backlog_repo_link_body: The repository name in the Repos Issues, PRs, and Actions headers now links straight to the repo on its forge.
prrow_in_train_title: a merge train is landing this PR
prrow_awaiting_approval_title: a workflow is awaiting approval to run — approve it on GitHub
feat_workflow_approval_flag_body: A pull request in the PRs tab now shows a "needs approval" chip when a GitHub Actions workflow on its head commit is awaiting manual approval to run — so you know to approve it on GitHub before CI can proceed.
gloss_epic_def: In Shepherd, a tracking issue whose sub-issues are wired by dependency edges — Shepherd spawns a session per ready child, collects their PRs on an integration branch, and lands everything as one final PR. (From Agile, where an epic is a large body of work split into smaller stories.)
gloss_pr_def: Pull request — a proposed set of code changes submitted for review and merging into a branch.
gloss_ci_def: Continuous integration — automatically building and testing every change so problems surface early.
gloss_critic_def: Shepherd's isolated, read-only review agent that inspects a PR's diff once CI is green and posts a verdict.
feat_glossary_body: Terms in What's-New and coachmarks now have a dashed underline — hover or tap to see what they mean, with a Wikipedia link for industry-standard terms.
feat_task_autopilot_body: The New Task dialog now has an "Autopilot to PR" checkbox. It starts from the repo's Autopilot setting, but you can turn it off for a single task you'd rather discuss and approve step by step — without changing the repo-wide default.
feat_newtask_infotips_body: Plan gate, Autopilot to PR, and Research task each got a small "i" — hover (or tap on mobile) to read what it does, instead of a paragraph crowding the dialog. Autopilot also shows the repo's standing default right beside it, so it's clear how the repo normally handles it before you change it for one task.
diagnostics_fix_confirm_body: Shepherd will run this command on the server as your user:
diagnostics_fix_action_claude_trust: Shepherd will mark this repo folder as trusted for Claude Code so the usage gauge can read it. No command runs.
diagnostics_fix_failed: Fix failed — the command did not complete. Try running it manually.
feat_diagnose_fix_body: When Settings → Diagnostics flags a missing prerequisite with a known install command — bun, node, herdr, or claude — you can now fix it in place: a Fix button runs the exact command (shown first for your confirmation) on the server and re-checks. Steps that need a login, like gh and tailscale, still link out to guidance.
feat_diagnose_doc_links_body: Diagnostics rows that need a manual step — GitHub CLI login, Tailscale setup, installing Shepherd to your home screen — now link straight to how-to docs.
diagnostics_fix_unresolved: The command ran, but the check is still not OK — it may need a manual step or a restart.
diagnostics_fix_unresolved_code: Shepherd updated the setting, but the check is still not OK — Claude Code may need a restart to pick it up.
feat_claude_folder_trust_body: Settings → Diagnostics now flags when Claude Code hasn't trusted your repo folder — which can leave the usage gauge blank — and offers a one-click fix that marks it trusted (no command runs). The check appears when Claude Code is installed and you're on subscription auth.
herd_rundown_title: Show the daily Herd Rundown digest
rundown_failed: Couldn't generate a rundown. Try regenerating it.
rundown_empty: No rundown yet — generate one to see what changed and what needs you.
rundown_main_hint: The rundown is shown in the herd panel on the left.
gloss_merge_train_def: Shepherd's queue that carries a ready PR through rebase and merge automatically, landing it once CI stays green.
gloss_rework_def: A session sent back to revise its work after the plan gate or PR critic requested changes, instead of approving it.
feat_herd_rundown_body: A new ☰ Rundown lens shows a daily digest of your herd: what landed overnight, what needs a decision, which sessions went CI-red or REWORK, the merge-train status, and what to focus on next. Each item deep-links to its session.
feat_subagent_fanout_body: The Activity tab now shows the sub-agents a session has spawned, live, with each one's type and how long it ran.
rundown_all_quiet: All quiet — nothing needs you right now.
syncfork_toast_diverged: Your fork's default branch has commits that aren't upstream — sync it manually on GitHub.
syncfork_toast_auth: Not logged in to GitHub. Run gh auth login.
syncfork_toast_generic: Couldn't sync the fork, please retry
feat_fork_sync_title: Keep a fork current with one click
feat_fork_sync_body: Fork repos in the picker now show a ⟲ Sync button. It runs gh repo sync to bring your fork's default branch up to date with upstream and fast-forwards the local clone — so new PR branches you cut start from current upstream instead of a stale base.
feat_usage_reset_countdown_body: The 5-hour and weekly usage gauges now show how long until the window resets — e.g. "resets in 2h (21:30)" — so you can see at a glance whether you'll have capacity soon.
clonerepo_no_repos: No new repositories to clone — you already have them all.
clonerepo_repos_unavailable: Couldn't list your GitHub repositories. Clone by URL below.
feat_clone_repo_picker_body: The clone dialog now lists every GitHub repo you can reach — your own account plus the orgs and teams you belong to — and clones the one you pick directly. Already-cloned repos are hidden, and clone-by-URL is still there as a fallback.
gitrail_review_skipped: Review not started — conditions changed or one's already running.
gitrail_review_failed: Couldn't start the review. Try again.
gitrail_review_plan_skipped: Couldn't start a plan review right now.
gitrail_review_plan_unavailable: Plan review not started: .shepherd-plan.md is missing, empty, or unreadable.
gitrail_review_plan_failed: Couldn't start the plan review. Try again.
feat_manual_critic_review_body: When the AI critic never started or stalled without a verdict, a Review/Re-review button on the PR rail lets you start or restart it on demand — for open PRs with green CI.
feat_manual_plan_review_body: When the plan gate stalls without a verdict (REVIEW ERR), a Review-plan button on the git rail lets you re-kick the adversarial plan review on demand — no need to open the plan panel.
unitrow_quota_rework_tip: Auto-fix retried up to its cap and stopped with work unfinished — open the session to take over.
unitrow_quota_review_tip: The auto-review loop stalled before reaching a verdict — open the session to resume or take over.
unitrow_quota_error_tip: The critic hit an error and this session is parked — open it to see what happened and retry.
status_ready_tip: This session's work is complete and its PR is green — ready to merge.
status_merging_tip: Shepherd is merging this PR now via the merge train.
feat_mobile_header_body: The status filter in the Herd header is now a compact segmented control on mobile — one tap switches lens, glyphs are gone, and the bar stays single-line at any phone width.
feat_repo_shortcuts_body: In the New Task pane you can now switch repositories from the keyboard without leaving the prompt: Alt+[ and Alt+] step through every repo, Alt+1–3 jump to your recent repos, and Alt+R opens the repo picker. On macOS use the Option (⌥) key.
newtask_upstream_behind: {count} behind origin — task will start from latest
newtask_upstream_diverged: Diverged from origin ({behind} behind, {ahead} ahead) — task will start from local {base}
feat_base_freshen_body: New tasks now start from the latest upstream tip of the base branch — Shepherd fetches and fast-forwards at launch, so you no longer fold in upstream changes by hand when the task is done. The New Task dialog flags when your base is behind or has diverged from origin.
feat_build_queue_collapse_body: The build queue can now be collapsed to a single bar so it no longer covers the terminal. Click the chevron in its header to fold or unfold it.
queuebadge_stale_title: Build queue: {total} steps, progress unreported — agent working but hasn't updated step status
queuebadge_stale_aria: Build queue progress unreported: {total} steps, agent working but status not updated
feat_build_queue_progress_title: Build queue progress at a glance
feat_build_queue_progress_body: Session cards now show a small amber badge with a progress meter when an approved build queue is active — see resolved / total steps without opening the panel.
learnings_optimizer_stalled_body: The rule optimizer failed {count} times in a row. Check the server logs.
feat_optimize_learnings_title: Fix ‘not working’ house rules in one click
feat_optimize_learnings_body: When a house rule stops preventing mistakes, filter to the flagged ones and let Shepherd reword them from the failures — individually or all at once.
feat_visual_recap_body: A finished session's recap now renders as a scannable document — a tree of what changed, the real annotated diff, and toned callouts — instead of flat text. Expand a recap to see it.
gloss_inferred_def: Derived by the recap model from the code — not verified against the real diff. Treat it as a hint, not ground truth.
feat_visual_recap_cards_body: Recaps now render schema changes, API endpoints, code excerpts, tables, and checklists as typed cards — not just flat text. Data-model and API cards are marked "inferred" to signal they were extracted by the model, not verified.
feat_visual_recap_diagrams_body: Recaps can now include rendered architecture and flow diagrams (Mermaid). They're marked "inferred" — extracted by the model, not verified against the diff.
vblock_wireframe_mockup_label: Illustrative mockup — not generated from code
feat_visual_recap_wireframes_body: Recaps of UI work can now include rendered, themed mockups of the affected screens — clearly labelled as illustrative, not screenshots.
learnings_triage_filter_active_aria: Showing only {repo} learnings; click to show all repos
learnings_about_budget: Each repo's approved rules are injected up to a shared character budget; rules over the budget are skipped until you trim them or raise the budget.
feat_learnings_triage_body: The Learnings drawer now floats over-budget and not-working repos to the top, pins each repo's header while you scroll, marks over-budget repos in amber, and adds a 'needs attention' jump bar — so you can see what to act on at a glance.
qform_sent: Answers sent to the planning agent.
qform_sent_undelivered: Couldn't reach the planning agent — it may have already moved on.
qform_submit_error: Couldn't send answers. Please try again.
planpanel_proposed_caption: Proposed — not yet built · the plan text below is authoritative
feat_visual_plan_body: Plan-gate plans can now render as a scannable visual document — file trees, diagrams, data models and more — shown above the plan text when the planning agent emits them.
feat_plan_question_answers_body: When a plan asks you open questions, you can now answer them right in the plan view — pick options or type a response and send them straight back to the planning agent to refine the plan.
feat_activity_visual_feed_title: Activity is now a visual feed
feat_activity_visual_feed_body: The Activity tab shows a live tree of changed files and a grouped tool stream while the agent works, then graduates to the full visual recap once the session settles.
feat_plan_reopen_body: Your signed-off plan stays reachable while work runs. Tap the PLAN chip on any executing session — row, tile, or viewport — to view it read-only, on desktop or mobile.
automation_lightweight_desc: Local git only — no Forge, no PRs. Branch squash-merged locally at completion.
automation_lightweight_detail: In lightweight mode Shepherd drives the repo with local git only: no GitHub, no pull requests, no remote. Auto-Drain, All-PRs Critic and Draft reconcile are unavailable. When the task finishes the agent's branch is squash-merged into the base branch locally; you push when you choose.
gloss_lightweight_repo_def: A repo Shepherd drives with local git only — no Forge, no GitHub, no PRs, no remote. When a task finishes, the agent's branch is squash-merged into the base branch locally; the operator pushes to a remote when they choose.
feat_lightweight_repo_body: Repos without a GitHub remote can now run in [[lightweight_repo|lightweight]] mode: Shepherd drives local git only — no PRs, no Forge. Enable it in the automation panel; the agent's branch is squash-merged locally at completion.
feat_issues_filter_mine_body: The Repos and New Task issue lists now hide issues assigned to other people by default, showing only unassigned issues or ones assigned to you. Open the Filters menu and turn off "mine & unassigned" to see everything; the setting is remembered across both lists.
diagnostics_hint_gitcap_old: git ≥ 2.38 not available — lightweight repo mode needs git merge-tree --write-tree (git ≥ 2.38) for local merges. git may be missing (see the git check) or too old; install or update it to use lightweight repos.
diagnostics_hint_gh_not_required: GitHub CLI is not installed or not authenticated, but no forge-backed repos are configured — gh is optional when using only lightweight repos.
feat_repo_settings_backlog_body: The per-repo automation settings — critic, plan gate, autopilot, auto-drain/merge, sandbox profile, default model, drain config and reviewer/merger roles — are now an Automation tab in the Repos drill-down. Tune any repo's defaults without launching a task; changes share one source of truth with the in-task panel.
toast_task_held: Task held — usage is high. It'll start when usage resets.
settings_usage_hold_enabled_label: Hold new tasks during high usage
settings_usage_hold_hint: When enabled, new manual tasks are paused while primary usage is at or above the threshold below. They start automatically when usage resets.
settings_usage_hold_enabled_save_failed: Couldn't change usage hold setting. Retry.
settings_usage_hold_pct_hint: Hold new tasks when primary usage reaches this percentage (0–100). Lower values hold tasks earlier.
settings_usage_hold_pct_save_failed: Couldn't change the usage hold threshold. Retry.
settings_fable_available_hint: When off, tasks that request Fable run on Opus (1M context) instead — use this while Fable is globally unavailable. The New Task picker hides Fable and stops defaulting to it.
feat_usage_aware_holding_title: Tasks pause automatically when usage is high
feat_usage_aware_holding_body: New tasks now pause automatically when your Claude usage is high — they start on their own when usage resets. Submit anyway to override, or turn it off in Settings → Session.
topbar_held_spawn_failed: Couldn't start the held task. It's still held — try again.
topbar_held_discard_failed: Couldn't discard the held task. Try again.
topbar_held_why: Held because usage is high — they start automatically when usage resets.
retry_continue_steer: Please continue — your usage limit should have reset.
retry_failed: Retry failed: no sessions resumed. Check the targets, then try again.
feat_usage_halt_retry_body: When sessions stop because your Claude usage limit was reached, a ⟳ Retry button appears once usage resets — it resumes all halted sessions and steers them to continue in one click.
learnings_merge_now_started: Looking for rules to merge in {repo}…
learnings_actions_help: The repo buttons start manual maintenance runs: “Suggest merges” looks for near-duplicate rules and groups them so you can fold each group into one rule while keeping its track record; “Distill now” scans recent sessions for signals and proposes new rules without waiting for the schedule.
learnings_recur_lead: These rules appear in several repos — consider adding one to your global CLAUDE.md.
settings_auto_optimize_flagged_help: When a rule starts failing, rewrite it once automatically before it becomes eligible for auto-retirement.
feat_learnings_auto_retire_body: Rules that consistently fail to help agents are now automatically retired. Review them in the Learnings drawer and restore any that were retired by mistake.
feat_ready_lens_hides_waiting_body: The Ready filter now hides sessions that are waiting on someone else — a PR handed off to a reviewer or merger, or one a merge train is already landing. They stay visible under the All filter.
issues_filter_active_title: Hide issues already being worked on (shepherd:active)
issues_filter_all_in_progress: All matching issues are in progress — toggle off to see them
feat_issues_filter_active_body: The Repos and New Task issue lists now offer a "hide in progress" filter in the Filters menu that hides issues already claimed by a running session (labeled shepherd:active). Off by default; the setting is remembered across both lists.
feat_ff_main_body: Fast-forward a repo's local default branch to origin any time from Repos — no PR merge required.
learnings_scoped_title: Glob-scoped rule — injected only for tasks whose target files match its globs, not every session. Keeps the budget for the rules a task actually needs.
feat_scoped_learnings_body: A learned rule can now carry glob patterns (e.g. src/**) so it's injected only for tasks touching those files — the 4000-char budget caps within the matched set instead of biting across the whole repo. Set or clear a rule's scope from the Learnings drawer.
feat_learnings_merge_body: A background pass now clusters a repo's near-duplicate house rules and surfaces them as merge suggestions in the Learnings drawer. Consolidate a group into one rule with a single click — the survivor keeps its track record and the rest are retired with a citation back to it.
feat_learnings_recur_body: When the same rule recurs across several repos, the Learnings drawer now suggests promoting it to your user-global CLAUDE.md — kept as a suggestion you can dismiss.
feat_open_linked_issue_body: GitRail now shows an "Issue ↗" link for tasks spawned from a backlog issue — jump straight to the issue on GitHub or Gitea.
feat_learnings_retire_push_body: When the daily background pass retires underperforming house rules, you now get a push notification so you learn of it without opening the Learnings drawer. Tap it to review the retired rules and restore any retired by mistake.
feat_learnings_repo_glyph_title: Repo at a glance in Learnings
feat_learnings_repo_glyph_body: Each repo's group in the Learnings drawer now shows its project emoji (or the ▣ marker) beside the name — just like session cards — so it's clear which repo a learning belongs to.
feat_ios_text_size_body: On iPhone and iPad, Shepherd's home-screen app now follows the system Text Size setting (Control Center, or Settings → Display & Brightness → Text Size). The whole interface scales with the slider — larger for readability or smaller for density — preserving the current look at the default position.
issues_filter_subissues_title: Hide native sub-issues (children of an epic) — start an epic drain instead
issues_filter_all_sub_issues: All matching issues are epic sub-issues — toggle off to see them, or start an epic drain
feat_issues_filter_subissues_body: The Repos and New Task issue lists now hide GitHub native sub-issues by default, so you start an epic drain from the parent instead of draining a child alone. Open the Filters menu and turn off "hide sub-issues" to show them; epic parents (including mid-level epics) stay visible. Remembered across both lists.
learnings_recur_promote_aria: Add this rule to your user-global CLAUDE.md
learnings_recur_promote_confirm: This writes the rule into ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md — a global, unversioned file applied to every repo. Shepherd owns this block and rewrites it on each promote.
learnings_recur_promote_done: Rule added to your global CLAUDE.md.
learnings_recur_promote_failed: Couldn't write to your global CLAUDE.md.
feat_learnings_promote_global_body: A cross-repo recurrence suggestion can now be written straight into your user-global ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md with one guarded click (a two-step confirm) — no PR, applied to every repo.
settings_reduced_push_hint: Mute every notification except a session waiting on you for 5s (usage and cost alerts still come through).
settings_reduced_push_disabled_note: Reduced mode: only ready-after-5s notifications are sent.
feat_reduced_push_body: A new global switch in Settings → Device silences every push notification except one: a session that's been waiting on you for 5 seconds. Usage and cost alerts still come through. Turn it on for a low-noise day where the only banner you get is "something needs you and didn't resolve itself".
feat_issue_filters_menu_title: Issue filters grouped into a menu
feat_issue_filters_menu_body: The Repos and New Task issue lists now gather their filters — mine & unassigned, hide in progress, hide sub-issues — into a single Filters menu with an active-count badge, instead of a row of chips.
learnings_trial_title: Auto-promoted on strong evidence — on trial until it proves helpful; injected at lowest priority and auto-removed if it underperforms.
learnings_revert_trial_aria: Revert this trial to the proposed queue
learnings_revert_failed: Couldn't revert the trial — try again.
gloss_trial_def: A proposed house rule auto-promoted to active on strong, multi-source evidence, injected at lowest priority while it proves itself. It is auto-removed if it underperforms (Wilson auto-retire) or stays inert, and can be reverted to the queue by hand.
feat_learnings_auto_trial_body: Strong, recurring rule proposals now auto-promote to an active [[trial|trial]] instead of waiting in the manual approval queue — and underperforming trials are removed automatically. Trials are badged and one-click revertible in the learnings drawer.
docagent_toast_observe: {repo}: doc-agent observe run — no PR opened
docagent_button_title: Update this repo's docs — opens a PR for review
docagent_observe_note: Observe mode — runs log changes but open no PR
docagent_trigger_skipped: Doc agent didn't start (already running or nothing to do)
feat_doc_agent_ui_body: Trigger the PR-gated doc agent for a repo from the Repos Actions bar. A status badge tracks the run and links the doc-update PR it opens; a popover shows recent runs. Opt-in via SHEPHERD_DOC_AGENT.
gloss_weighted_units_def: A model-weighted measure of token spend that counts what actually draws down your subscription limits — output tokens cost far more than cached reads, so weighted units, not raw token counts, reflect true usage.
usage_overhead_bykind_caption: Counts every satellite pass in range — including herd-wide rundowns and repo-wide doc passes — so this differs from the Spend view's per-task figure.
feat_usage_satellite_by_type_body: The Usage → Overhead lens now breaks satellite passes down by type — review, plan gate, recap, rundown, and doc agent — with a pass count and weighted-unit share for each.
gloss_satellite_pass_def: An automated LLM pass Shepherd spawns alongside the main task agent — critic / PR-review, plan-gate, recap, rundown, or doc-agent. Its token spend is real overhead attributed back to the task, on top of the agent's own authoring.
usage_timeline_empty: No usage recorded in this range yet
feat_repo_switch_retarget_body: Picking a repo from the chip rail now jumps the terminal to that repo too — to the session waiting on your answer, or else the first active one. No more staring at a session from the repo you just left.
feedback_dialog_details_placeholder_bug: What went wrong? What did you expect to happen?
feedback_dialog_details_placeholder_feat: What would you like to see, and why?
feedback_dialog_note: Opens a prefilled GitHub issue in a new tab — review and edit before submitting.
settings_feedback_blurb: Found a bug or have an idea? Let us know.
feat_feedback_body: Found a bug or have an idea? Use the gear menu or Settings → Device to report a bug, request a feature, or send feedback — it opens a prefilled GitHub issue.
feat_usage_dashboard_body: See where your tokens go — spend by repo and task, the cache-vs-generation overhead split, and live limit burn-down. Open it from the gear menu.
feat_review_inflight_body: When a critic or plan-gate review is running, the terminal warns you before an incoming directive can collide with your typing — and tells you the outcome when it finishes.
