reviewbanner_calm: A review is running — it will steer this session when it finishes. Wait before typing.
reviewbanner_escalated: You're typing — an incoming directive may collide with your input. Consider waiting.
reviewbanner_pasted: Review done — directive pasted into this session.
reviewbanner_awaiting_go: Plan approved — nothing pasted, awaiting your Go.
feat_usage_limits_trend_body: The Limits lens now shows a real recorded trend, not just a projection — a current-cycle sparkline under each gauge plus an expandable history panel that charts how your 5h, weekly, and credit usage moved over past reset cycles.
feat_spend_per_task_dollars_body: The Spend lens now shows a dollar amount for each task — not just per repository and the grand total — so you can spot your top-spending tasks at a glance. Shown only in API-key mode.
hold_halted_error: Halted on an error — needs you.
hold_halted_usage: Paused at the usage limit — resumes at {time}.
hold_blocked_stall: Quiet — no recent activity; may be stuck.
hold_quota_rework: Auto-fix hit its limit — open findings still need you.
hold_quota_review: Critic keeps finding issues — auto-review paused.
hold_quota_error: Critic can't review this PR — needs you.
hold_quota_plan: Plan review stuck — keeps requesting changes.
hold_plan_rework: Plan review wants changes (round {round}/{cap}) — your call.
hold_plan_question: The plan has questions waiting on your answer.
hold_critic_rework: Critic requested changes ({findings} open) — steered back to the agent.
hold_ci_red: CI is failing — needs a fix.
hold_awaiting_merge: Ready and handed to a merger.
hold_train_error: Merge train hit an error — needs you.
hold_stalled: Quiet — no recent activity; may be stuck.
hold_recap_attention: Recap flagged this for your attention.
hold_ready_merge: Ready to merge — waiting on you.
feat_why_parked_body: Held, blocked and gate-stuck sessions now show a one-line reason on the card — so you can see why a session needs you at a glance.
feat_usage_task_names_body: The per-task rows in the Usage → Spend and Overhead lenses now show each task's short name alongside its designation — so you can tell tasks apart at a glance instead of reading IDs.
firsttask_confirm_intro: Shepherd hasn't driven {repo} before. Review the automation settings below — they apply to every task on this repo — then confirm to start this one.
feat_first_task_confirm_title: Review settings before the first task
feat_first_task_confirm_body: Starting a task on a new repo now shows a quick review step so you can check automation settings before the first run. After confirming once, future tasks on the same repo start without interruption.
toast_broadcast_result: Broadcast · {delivered} steered now, {queued} queued on busy agents (act after current turn), {offline} unreachable.
issuerow_assigned_notice: Assigned to {who} — you can still start
feat_issue_assignee_title: See who's assigned to each issue
feat_issue_assignee_body: When the "mine & unassigned" issue filter is off, each backlog issue now shows a chip for every assignee, so you can tell at a glance who already owns a piece of work.
integrated_epics_land_confirm_prompt: Merge the landing PR and close the epic?
integrated_epics_land_confirm_migration_: {count} migration(s) detected — they were never run; verify before landing.
integrated_epics_land_not_ready_ci_faili: CI is failing on the landing PR
integrated_epics_land_not_ready_computin: Still computing — checks pending or mergeability resolving
integrated_epics_land_not_ready_conflict: Landing PR has conflicts and is not mergeable
integrated_epics_land_not_ready_generic: Landing PR is not ready to merge yet
integrated_epics_land_failed: Couldn't land the epic — try again
feat_land_epic_title: Land integrated epics from the app
feat_land_epic_body: When an epic's landing PR is open and all checks pass, a new 'Land epic' button in the Integrated-epics band lets you merge it with one click — no need to switch to GitHub.
feat_epic_auto_land_body: With auto-merge enabled for a repo, an integrated epic's landing PR now merges automatically once it's clean and all checks pass — the same opt-in that lands your full-auto sessions. Epics that touch migrations are held for manual review, and the 'Land epic' button still works any time.
landed_epic_toast: Epic #{number} landed — merged to the default branch
rundown_epic_open_aria: Go to epic #{number} to land it
feat_rundown_epics_to_land_body: The daily Herd Rundown now escalates integrated epics whose landing PR is ready to merge as a top-priority item — even when no session is live — so a forgotten last-mile landing no longer slips. Click it to jump straight to the epic's Land button.
feat_landing_auto_repair_body: When an epic's landing PR stays red after automatic CI reruns, Shepherd now dispatches one capped agent session that fixes CI and pushes directly to the integration branch — falling back to the operator surface if it can't.
settings_tui_fullscreen_hint: Opt the main agent session into Claude Code's fullscreen renderer for flatter memory on long autonomous runs. Research preview — behavior may change. Applies to newly started or resumed sessions, not ones already running. Also disables mouse capture so the web terminal stays usable.
settings_tui_fullscreen_save_failed: Couldn't change the fullscreen renderer setting.
settings_tui_disable_mouse_hint: Stop Claude Code from capturing the mouse in the agent terminal. Applies to newly started or resumed sessions.
feat_tui_fullscreen_body: You can now opt agent sessions into Claude Code's fullscreen renderer from Settings → Session, for flatter memory on long autonomous runs. It's a research preview (off by default) and applies to newly started or resumed sessions.
feat_decommission_on_merge_body: After you merge a session's PR, the confirmation toast now offers a one-click Decommission to tear the finished session down — no hunting for the button.
unitrow_manual_steps_link: View these steps on the Owed lens
feat_manual_operator_steps_body: Declare manual steps in a PR's shepherd:manual-steps block (flip a flag, set an env var, run a backfill) and Shepherd surfaces them as an amber chip on the session row and a checklist in the Done recap — so they aren't lost when the PR lands.
unitrow_ack_manual_steps_failed: Couldn't acknowledge the manual steps — try again.
hold_manual_steps: {steps} manual step(s) to do before merge — ack to proceed.
feat_manual_operator_steps_gate_body: A PR with an un-acknowledged manual step (anything not marked POST-MERGE) is now held out of auto-merge until you act. Click "Ack steps" on the session row to clear the gate; you're nudged via push and the daily rundown. POST-MERGE-only steps never block.
automation_manual_steps_issue_name: Track manual steps in an issue
automation_manual_steps_issue_desc: On merge, open a GitHub issue listing the PR's manual operator steps
automation_prewarm_epic_ci_desc: Opens the epic's landing PR early as a draft so each child merge re-runs the landing CI against main. Only helps if this repo's CI runs on draft PRs and re-runs on push; a red draft is visible on GitHub until the epic completes.
herd_owed_title: Post-merge manual steps you still owe
herd_owed_count: {count} merged PR(s) still owe post-merge steps
owed_main_hint: Owed post-merge steps are shown in the herd panel on the left.
owed_empty: No post-merge steps owed — you're all caught up.
owed_note: These are every manual step the merged PRs declared. P2 tracks acknowledgement, not per-step completion, so steps you already did before merge appear here too — tick them to clear them.
owed_dismiss_title: Clear all owed steps for this PR
owed_frozen_pre_merge_note: These steps will be tracked here once the PR merges.
owed_frozen_cleared_note: No longer owed — completed or dismissed.
owed_frozen_unknown_note: Couldn't load owed status; showing the steps from this session's PR.
feat_manual_steps_post_merge_body: After a PR with manual steps merges and the session archives, the steps live on in the new Owed lens — tick each off as you do it, and they persist until done (well past the Done window). Enable "Track manual steps in an issue" per repo to also open a GitHub tracking issue on merge, linked back to the PR.
feat_manual_steps_chip_title: Jump from a chip to its owed steps
feat_manual_steps_chip_body: The "N manual step(s)" chip on a session is now a link — click it to open the Owed lens and jump straight to that session's steps.
feat_steer_repo_binding_body: Saved steers can now be limited to specific repos. Open Settings → Saved steers and use each steer's Repos control to pick where it appears — its steer-bar chip and backlog quick-button then show only on those repos. Leave it on "All repos" to keep a steer everywhere.
integrated_epics_rebase_paused_cap: Couldn't auto-rebase after repeated tries — over to you
integrated_epics_rebase_paused_conflict: Landing PR has a real conflict — over to you
integrated_epics_rebase_paused_driver: Merge driver unavailable on the server — over to you
feat_auto_rebase_landing_prs_body: When an epic's landing PR falls behind its base, Shepherd now rebases it automatically. If it hits a conflict, a retry cap, or a missing merge driver, a warn chip on the band row tells you exactly why and hands off to you.
login_subtitle: This instance is protected. Enter the operator password to continue.
settings_logout_hint: End this session on this device. You'll need the operator password to sign back in.
feat_operator_auth_body: Shepherd now sits behind a single-operator password. Every page, the live terminal, and the activity stream require a session — set SHEPHERD_PASSWORD, or grab the auto-generated one from the server log on first boot. Sign out anytime from Settings → Session.
newtask_agent_provider_codex_alpha_note: Codex integration is an Alpha/MVP feature and is under heavy development.
newtask_agent_provider_codex_suggested_f: Claude Code is at the usage-hold threshold, so running this task on Codex sidesteps the hold. Switch the coding CLI back to Claude Code to hold it until the reset or submit anyway instead.
newtask_agent_provider_codex_note: Codex starts as an interactive terminal session, running unattended under --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox (sandbox profile forced to trusted). Plan Gate is available. Autopilot is best-effort (Alpha) and drives an isolated session toward an open PR. The model picker uses Codex CLI models.
settings_default_agent_provider_hint: Chooses the interactive CLI preselected for new tasks. Claude Code keeps all Shepherd automation; Codex starts as a steerable terminal session while CLI-specific automation is still being wired.
settings_default_agent_provider_save_fai: Couldn't change default coding CLI. Retry.
settings_upnext_skip_cli_picker_label: Skip the coding-CLI picker in Up Next
settings_upnext_skip_cli_picker_hint: When enabled, Up Next quick-start launches with your default coding CLI immediately, without asking which CLI to use — even when more than one is installed.
settings_upnext_skip_cli_picker_save_fai: Couldn't change the Up Next picker setting. Retry.
settings_cli_claude_hint: Claude-backed runs use these effort, language, and authentication settings.
settings_cli_codex_hint: Codex runs through the local Codex CLI in a steerable terminal session. Alpha/MVP, under heavy development.
settings_cli_codex_auth_hint: Codex currently uses the local CLI login available to the server process.
feat_codex_cli_body: New tasks can now run through the local Codex CLI as an Alpha/MVP provider path. Choose Codex in the task dialog or set it as the default under Settings → Coding CLI; Codex starts as a steerable terminal session while CLI-specific automation is still being wired.
feat_held_task_cli_handoff_title: Hand off held tasks to another CLI
feat_held_task_cli_handoff_body: Held tasks now show which coding CLI they were originally queued for. Open the held-tasks popover, switch Run on to another provider, and Spawn now to hand the task to a CLI that still has room.
feat_codex_model_picker_body: The New Task dialog now switches the model menu with the selected coding CLI. Pick Codex to choose GPT/Codex models such as gpt-5.5, and Shepherd passes that model through to the Codex CLI.
diagnostics_hint_claude_optional: Claude Code CLI is optional because Codex is available. Install it if you want both agent runtimes.
diagnostics_hint_codex_missing: Codex CLI not found in PATH. Install Codex or Claude Code to run agent sessions.
diagnostics_hint_codex_optional: Codex CLI is optional because Claude Code is available. Install it if you want both agent runtimes.
diagnostics_hint_codex_model_chatgpt_inc: A configured Codex model isn't supported by your ChatGPT-account Codex login and is being replaced with the account default. Pick a supported model, or sign in to Codex with an API key.
taskid_button_title: Actions for {desig} — copy ID or get a prompt recommendation
recommend_loading_hint: A second agent is reading this session's terminal history. This can take a moment.
recommend_inject_failed: Couldn't inject the prompt into the session.
recommend_err_no_history: No terminal history to analyze — the session isn't live.
recommend_err_spawn_failed: Couldn't start the analysis agent. Check that the chosen CLI is installed.
recommend_err_timeout: The analysis didn't return a recommendation. Try again.
recommend_err_unavailable: Prompt recommendations are unavailable right now.
feat_taskid_recommend_title: Click a task ID for actions
feat_taskid_recommend_body: The task ID on each card is now a button. Click it to copy the ID, or have a second agent (Opus or GPT-5.5) analyze the session's terminal history and recommend the next prompt — ready to copy or inject straight into the session.
topbar_held_why_manual: Held because usage is high. They stay held until you start them manually.
feat_held_autostart_body: The held-tasks popover now has an auto-start checkbox. Leave it on and held tasks start automatically when usage resets, as before. Turn it off and they stay queued until you start each one manually — handy when you want to control exactly what runs after a reset.
feat_held_edit_title: Edit a held task before it runs
feat_held_edit_body: Each held task now has an Edit button that reopens the original New Task dialog, pre-filled with everything the task was queued with — prompt, repo, base branch, model, agent, attachments and run settings. Fix a typo or change the target before it spawns; your edits are saved back and the task stays held.
feat_herdr_update_session_list_title: See and visit the sessions an update would end
feat_herdr_update_session_list_body: The herdr-update dialog no longer just shows a count of running sessions — it lists each one, and every row jumps straight to that session. Wrap them up one by one, then update without worrying you've lost work.
automation_drain_fields_intro: These dials govern Auto-Drain and apply only while it's on:
automation_drain_fields_intro_epic: These dials still govern the running epic's draining — adjust them to retune it in flight:
drain_cap_hint: Most auto-started agents allowed to run at once. Once it's reached, Shepherd waits for a slot to free before picking up the next issue.
drain_label_hint: Only issues carrying this label get picked up — tag the tickets you want worked automatically with it.
drain_ceiling_hint: Pause new auto-starts once your account usage reaches this percentage. A higher value lets the drain keep going longer; the default is 80.
feat_diagram_lightbox_body: Click any diagram in a plan to open it near full-screen, then zoom and drag to read the fine labels. The plan view is also wider on desktop, so diagrams, data-model cards and diffs get more room.
backlog_filter_all_hidden: All repos hidden — show hidden to reveal
feat_hide_repos_body: Declutter the Backlog repos panel: hover a repo row and click the eye to hide it. A "Hidden" chip reveals hidden repos so you can bring them back. It's list-only — the repo's sessions, drain and totals are untouched.
files_empty: No files in the scratchpad yet.
feat_files_created_column_title: Sort scratchpad & worktree files by creation date
feat_files_created_column_body: The Files tab now shows when each file and folder was created, in a Created column you can sort by — click the Name or Created header to reorder the list, newest or oldest first.
feat_scratchpad_files_body: A live session's viewport now has a Files tab whenever its scratchpad holds artifacts. Browse the folders the agent wrote and download any file straight to your machine.
feat_codex_usage_topbar_body: The topbar usage popover now includes Codex token telemetry when a local Codex CLI is configured, so Claude and Codex capacity are visible from the same place.
session_autopilot_unavailable_title: Codex autopilot needs an isolated worktree session; this session shares its working directory, so autopilot stands down.
feat_codex_autopilot_body: Autopilot-until-PR is now available for Codex tasks. Toggle it on in New Task and Codex runs unattended toward an open PR. It is best-effort (Alpha): it drives isolated sessions only and stops at the open PR — it never auto-merges.
restore_cannot: Can't bring this one back — relaunch it instead
restore_branch_gone: Its branch was merged and removed — relaunch instead
restore_branch_in_use: Its branch is checked out elsewhere
feat_bring_back_body: Select any session in the Done lens and click Bring back to resume it — Shepherd restores your committed branch work and continues the conversation from where it left off. Uncommitted edits are not recovered.
herd_next_title: Up Next — a cross-repo ranked queue of un-started work to start
upnext_empty: You're all caught up — nothing queued to start.
upnext_main_hint: Up Next is shown in the herd panel on the left.
feat_upnext_title: Up Next — what to start, across every repo
feat_upnext_body: A new top-level lens ranks un-started GitHub issues across all your repos into one flat queue, priority-first. See the single most worthwhile thing to start next and spawn a session on it — or several — in one click.
feat_up_next_sort_body: Up Next now lets you switch between recommended ranking, newest, oldest, and title A-Z/Z-A order. Priority work stays grouped first, and your sort choice is remembered.
feat_newtask_wider_body: The New Task pane is now wider, giving the prompt and the Seed From issues list more room. Issue rows show more of the title, and up to two labels at a glance instead of one.
feat_revive_stranded_title: Revive sessions stranded by a herdr restart
feat_revive_stranded_body: When the herdr daemon restarts (a reboot, crash, or failed update), it restores your panes as bare shells — the agents inside are gone. Shepherd now spots these stranded sessions, flags each card with an "agent died — revive" action, and shows one banner to revive them all at once. Turn on auto-revive in Settings to have Shepherd bring default-account agents back automatically.
feat_merge_pr_context_menu_title: Merge a PR straight from the session list
feat_merge_pr_context_menu_body: When a session's pull request is ready to merge, its right-click (or long-press on touch) menu now has a Merge PR action right at the top — no need to open the session first. Click once to arm, again to confirm, and Shepherd merges it for you.
feat_terminal_font_size_body: The terminal wrench menu now has an experimental Text size stepper. Bump the agent output up or down — handy when it gets squinty, especially on mobile. Your choice is remembered across sessions on this device.
feat_epic_stall_legibility_title: See why an epic is waiting
feat_epic_stall_legibility_body: The epic panel now warns when an epic has no dependency order (so every child drains in parallel) and, when a train holds, spells out why — which child or gate is blocking new starts instead of just looking frozen.
feat_herd_resizable_body: Drag the splitter between the Herd sidebar and the terminal to choose how wide the session rail sits. Your width is remembered across refreshes; double-click the splitter to snap back to the default.
feat_manual_plan_rereview_body: The Re-review plan action now always starts a fresh review — even when the plan hasn't changed since the last round — so you can get a new verdict on a plan that stalled at the review cap. Trigger it from the plan menu, the git rail, or the plan panel. When a plan is already approved, the control now shows as unavailable with a reason instead of doing nothing.
feat_title_tap_toggle_body: A single tap on the session title now opens or closes the disclosure beside it: on desktop that's the PR, merge & critic actions, and on phone/touch it folds or unfolds the header to reclaim space for the terminal. Double-tap still renames the session.
feat_upnext_skip_cli_picker_title: Skip the coding-CLI picker in Up Next
feat_upnext_skip_cli_picker_body: A new opt-in setting (Settings → Coding CLI) launches Up Next quick-start with your default coding CLI immediately, instead of asking which CLI to use each time.
feat_add_repo_title: Add a repo from the Backlog
feat_add_repo_body: The Backlog repos panel now has a "+ Add repo" button — create a new project, clone, or fork a repo without leaving the browse view or touching the command line. The new repo is selected automatically when it's ready.
feat_plugin_ui_body: Plugins can now render quota meters, status badges and tables in Settings → Plugins instead of a raw JSON dump.
feat_plugin_ui_charts_body: Plugins can now publish gauges, sparklines, time-series, bar charts and timelines in Settings → Plugins — not just meters, badges and tables.
feat_plugin_gear_item_body: Plugins can now add a single item to the gear menu — opening a link, running a plugin action, or jumping to the plugin's panel.
feat_decommission_update_local_body: When you merge an isolated session's PR, the confirmation toast now offers one action that tears the session down and fast-forwards your local default branch in a single click.
codexupdate_instructions: Updates the codex on your PATH, using whichever installer manages it. Already-running codex panes keep their version; only new sessions pick up the update. Shepherd stays up.
codexupdate_done_ok: Updated to {latest}. New codex sessions will use it.
codexupdate_done_fail: Update did not complete (still on {current}). Check the log; you can retry.
codexupdate_done_fail_stuck: The update ran, but the codex on PATH still reports {current} (at {path}). If you have more than one codex install, update the one at that path directly — otherwise it may already be the newest on its release channel. See the log.
feat_codex_update_body: Shepherd now watches npm for a newer @openai/codex and shows a badge when one ships — like the herdr badge. One click runs the update; running codex panes are not interrupted.
feat_steer_context_menu_title: Right-click a steer to edit or run it
feat_steer_context_menu_body: Right-click a steer chip in the bar to open a small menu: run it (the same as a tap) or jump straight into the editor focused on that steer.
experiment_compare_hint: Spawn a read-only agent that reviews each variant's committed branch and writes a comparison + recommendation.
experiment_compare_running_hint: Wait until every variant has stopped — the comparison reads committed branch state, so a still-running run would compare a stale or empty diff.
experiment_action_failed: Could not start that run — please try again.
feature_model_comparison_title: Compare models & CLIs on one task
feature_model_comparison_body: From a session's menu, run the same task again as a parallel variant on a different model or CLI, or continue the same worktree with another CLI. Then hit Compare to spawn a read-only run that reviews every variant and recommends the best.
settings_role_models_hint: Pick the CLI and model for each helper agent Shepherd spawns. "Follows default" defers to the default CLI + model above; the effective environment is shown under each row.
settings_role_models_classifier_cost_hin: These run constantly and are kept on a small, fast model on purpose. Setting them to a heavy model can sharply raise cost.
settings_role_model_save_failed: Couldn't change the role environment. Retry.
settings_role_model_planner_hint: Adversarially reviews an agent's plan before it writes code.
settings_role_model_critic_hint: Reviews open PRs and steers findings back to the agent. Covers both the session critic and the repo-wide standalone critic.
settings_critic_effort_weakened_warning: The critic is a rigor role — a reduced reasoning effort weakens PR review. Keep it at high or above.
settings_role_model_namer_hint: Names a new session from its prompt.
settings_role_model_autopilot_hint: Decides when autopilot should pause for the operator.
feat_role_models_body: Settings → Coding CLI now lets you pick the CLI and model for each helper agent Shepherd spawns — planner, critic, doc agent, recap, and the namer/autopilot classifiers. Run each on Claude Code (Opus/Sonnet/…) or Codex (GPT-5.5/…), or let it follow your default environment.
feat_usage_downgrade_body: Settings → Coding CLI can now keep work flowing when usage runs high: instead of pausing, every spawned agent switches to a cheaper model once usage crosses a threshold you set — sitting below the usage-hold threshold so it downgrades first, then holds.
settings_usage_downgrade_hint: When enabled, every spawned agent (your main task sessions and the role agents) runs on the cheaper model below once primary usage reaches the threshold — work keeps going instead of pausing. Set the threshold below the usage-hold threshold so it downgrades first, then holds.
settings_usage_downgrade_enabled_save_fa: Couldn't change the usage downgrade setting. Retry.
settings_usage_downgrade_pct_hint: Switch spawns to the downgrade model when primary usage reaches this percentage (0–100). Keep it below the usage-hold threshold.
settings_usage_downgrade_pct_save_failed: Couldn't change the usage downgrade threshold. Retry.
settings_usage_downgrade_model_hint: The model every spawn uses while usage is above the threshold. Pick a cheaper model than your default (e.g. Haiku).
settings_usage_downgrade_model_save_fail: Couldn't change the downgrade model. Retry.
feat_taskid_copy_payload_title: Copy ID now carries the task's details
feat_taskid_copy_payload_body: Copying a task ID now puts its repo, branch and worktree path on the clipboard alongside the id. Paste it into a coding agent and it knows exactly what the task is and where its work lives — no lookup, no wasted tokens researching.
github_lens_intro: GitHub limits REST and GraphQL access in separate hourly budgets. When one runs out, the features that depend on it pause until it resets — the other keeps working.
github_lens_graphql_paused: GraphQL budget exhausted — PR, issue and backlog updates are paused for ~{time}.
github_lens_rest_paused: REST budget exhausted — CI status and merge actions are paused for ~{time}.
feat_github_rate_limits_body: The Usage view has a new GitHub tab showing your REST and GraphQL API budgets separately. When one is exhausted it explains which features pause until reset — so an empty GraphQL budget no longer looks like a silent breakage.
feat_owed_badge_body: The OWED Herd lens now shows a count badge when merged PRs still owe post-merge manual steps — so the lens earns your attention only when there's something to do, instead of looking identical whether 0 or 5 steps are outstanding.
github_lens_graphql_backoff: GraphQL polling is paused while a rate-limit backoff clears — PR, issue and backlog updates resume in ~{time}.
docagent_toast_error: {repo}: doc update aborted — prettier couldn't format the docs
feat_scratchpad_upload_body: You can now upload files directly into a session's scratchpad from the Files tab — click Upload or drag-and-drop files onto the list. The agent can read anything you drop there.
feat_newtask_hide_hidden_title: Hidden repos stay out of the New Task picker
feat_newtask_hide_hidden_body: Repos you've hidden no longer clutter the repo picker when you start a new task. They're dropped from the list and the recents shortcuts, but still appear the moment you search for one by name — so a hidden repo is always one keystroke away when you need it.
feat_usage_timeline_title: See your token usage over time
feat_usage_timeline_body: The Usage panel has a new Timeline tab: a day-by-hour heatmap of weighted-unit consumption across the selected range. Hotter hours read darker, so you can spot your high- and low-usage periods at a glance — including activity from sessions running right now.
demoribbon_blurb: Backend is simulated — nothing here is real infrastructure.
experiment_continue_scope: Keeps this Shepherd session, branch and worktree. Only the live agent process changes.
experiment_continue_mode_resume_hint: Loads the original instructions into the selected CLI.
experiment_continue_mode_summarize_hint: Read the worktree, write a short summary, then wait for new instructions.
feat_continue_with_handoff_title: Continue a session with another CLI
feat_continue_with_handoff_body: The old Replace action is now Continue with. It keeps the same Shepherd session and worktree, lets you pick the next CLI/model, and can either load the original task or ask the new agent to summarize first and wait.
prbadge_draft_unsupported: Draft state changes are not available for this forge
prbadge_ready_needs_signoff: Draft mode is waiting for sign-off. Submit an approving PR review or change the sign-off setting before marking it Ready for Review.
prbadge_merge_failed: Merge failed: {reason}. Check the PR, then retry.
feat_pr_badge_merge_title: Merge PRs from the badge menu
feat_pr_badge_merge_body: The PR badge menu on session cards now offers Merge PR whenever the pull request is actually mergeable — open, not a draft, no conflicts, checks not failing. Tap once to arm, tap again to confirm, and the PR merges without leaving the Herd view.
feat_pr_badge_menu_body: Session cards now open a compact PR menu from the PR badge. Open the pull request in a new tab, or flip an open PR between Draft and Ready for Review without leaving the Herd view.
viewport_preview_setup_sent: Preview setup sent to {name}; future starts will use the local repo script
viewport_preview_already_running: Dev server is already running; opening preview
feat_preview_local_script_body: The Preview start button now creates and reuses a repo-local script under the repo's Git metadata. Later sessions can start the same dev server without spending agent tokens, and Shepherd reuses an already-running server instead of starting another copy.
feat_repo_chip_pin_body: Right-click or hold a repo chip to pin that repo at the left edge of the herd filter. Pinning keeps the normal click-to-filter behavior and stays on this device.
feat_repo_chip_add_filter_title: Filter from the repo chip menu
feat_repo_chip_add_filter_body: Long-press or right-click a repo chip and choose "Add to filter" to narrow the herd to that repo — the same as Shift-clicking a chip on desktop, now reachable on touch.
feat_repo_chip_automation_settings_body: Right-click or long-press a repo chip and open Repo automation settings to change that repository's defaults without opening a task.
model_guidance_auto: Auto lets Shepherd seed manual tasks while unattended spawns stay on the provider default.
model_guidance_default: Provider default. Use it when you want the CLI vendor to choose the current baseline.
model_guidance_inherit: Inherits the global default for this repo or role.
model_guidance_default_context_note: Pinned choices also affect unattended drain and autopilot starts.
model_guidance_classifier_context_note: Classifier roles run often. Keep them cheap unless accuracy is failing.
model_guidance_downgrade_context_note: A downgrade target should be cheaper than your normal tier; Haiku is the intended cap-saving choice.
model_guidance_unknown: Relative cost guidance only. Actual spend depends on auth mode, account, and token volume.
model_guidance_claude_fable: Use for the hardest long-running work. Highest quality, highest unattended burn.
model_guidance_claude_opus: Use for PR criticism, planning, or risky refactors that need stronger judgment than Sonnet.
model_guidance_claude_opus_1m: Use only when the transcript or repo context truly needs 1M context.
model_guidance_claude_sonnet: Balanced default for everyday coding, reviews, and recaps.
model_guidance_claude_sonnet_1m: Sonnet behavior with 1M context. Reserve it for large-context runs.
model_guidance_claude_haiku: Cheap and fast. Best for naming and classifiers, weak for primary coding.
model_guidance_codex_55: Use when you want the strongest Codex run and the task can justify premium burn.
model_guidance_codex_56_sol: Latest frontier Codex model. Choose it manually for ambitious coding, research, and document work.
model_guidance_codex_56_terra: Balanced GPT-5.6 Codex option for everyday agentic coding work.
model_guidance_codex_56_luna: Fast and affordable GPT-5.6 Codex option for lighter or high-volume work.
model_guidance_codex_54: Strong Codex tier for hard implementation and review work.
model_guidance_codex_53: Balanced Codex coding default for most implementation tasks.
model_guidance_codex_51_codex: Cheaper Codex-specific fallback when throughput matters more than peak reasoning.
model_guidance_codex_5_codex: Budget Codex-specific option for small edits and routine follow-ups.
model_guidance_codex_51: General GPT-5.1 profile. Use when you do not need the Codex-tuned variant.
model_guidance_codex_5: Budget general GPT-5 profile for light tasks.
model_guidance_codex_o3: Reasoning-heavy option. Good for analysis, expensive for routine coding.
feat_model_cost_guidance_body: Model menus now show relative cost markers and task-fit tags, and the selected model explains when it is worth using. The guidance appears in New Task, Settings, repo defaults and the compare or continue picker.
feat_gpt_56_codex_models_body: The Codex model picker now includes GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna as manual choices while keeping GPT-5.5 as the default for unattended-safe starts.
feat_tab_signaling_title: Your browser tab now signals the herd
feat_tab_signaling_body: When Shepherd is open in a background tab, the tab title shows a count of sessions needing you, and the favicon takes on a severity color — red for failing CI, amber for blocked, green for ready to merge. A brief check mark appears when the herd drains. Installed as an app, it also updates the app badge.
feat_plan_question_signal_title: Plan questions now show in the tab signal
feat_plan_question_signal_body: When a plan gate asks you questions during planning, the background tab now counts it — amber, alongside blocked sessions — so an unanswered plan question no longer waits unseen. It clears as soon as you answer.
telemetry_consent_body: Shepherd can send anonymous usage [[telemetry|telemetry]] — your OS, version, and which features are used. No code, file paths, repo names, or personal data ever leave your machine. You can change this any time in Settings.
settings_telemetry_hint: Send anonymous, aggregate usage data (OS, version, feature usage) to help prioritise Shepherd's roadmap. No code or personal data.
settings_telemetry_unavailable: Disabled by DO_NOT_TRACK or not configured on this server.
gloss_telemetry_def: Automatic collection of anonymous usage and diagnostic data from software, sent back to its developers to guide improvements.
gloss_host_capacity_def: Whether Shepherd's systemd service (or its slice) sets a memory or CPU ceiling — MemoryHigh, MemoryMax, or CPUQuota. Without one, a burst of concurrent sessions can consume all the host's RAM or CPU and starve the box; the Diagnostics check warns until a limit is set.
gloss_herdr_hygiene_def: Shepherd reconciles herdr's panes and processes against its own session model to spot leftovers. It counts panes with live leftover processes — not systemd's 'Tasks' figure for the herdr service, which counts threads (each agent process spawns many), so a Tasks count in the thousands is normal and not by itself a process leak.
feat_anonymous_telemetry_body: Shepherd can now send anonymous, privacy-first usage data (OS, version, feature usage — never code or personal data) to help prioritise the roadmap. It's off until you opt in, respects DO_NOT_TRACK, and can be toggled any time in Settings.
commandbar_placeholder: Jump to a session, repo, or lens — or run a command or search docs…
feat_command_bar_title: Command bar — jump to anything with ⌘K
feat_command_bar_body: Press ⌘K (macOS) or Ctrl+K (Windows/Linux) — even while the terminal is focused — to open a quick-switcher. Type to fuzzy-search across your sessions, repositories, and herd lenses — matched letters are highlighted, and sessions match on their description too — then hit Enter to jump straight there.
settings_tab_ticker_hint: When a background tab needs you, show grouped glyph counts (⚠ CI failed · ✋ blocked · ✓ ready · ▶ running) instead of a single number. Off by default.
settings_hide_info_tips_hint: Remove the ⓘ info icons and the dashed-underline glossary term explanations across the app. Status chip and badge tooltips are not affected. Off by default.
feat_hide_info_tips_body: Know your way around? Settings → Device now has a Hide info tooltips switch that strips the ⓘ info icons and the dashed-underline glossary terms from the whole app. Status chip and badge tooltips stay put, so nothing becomes unreadable. Off by default.
feat_tab_glyph_title_body: Turn on a denser background-tab title that groups what needs you by glyph — ⚠ CI failed, ✋ blocked, ✓ ready, ▶ running — plus a progress ring on the favicon for the session you're watching. Enable it in Settings → Device.
commandbar_cmd_decommission_kw: decommission archive remove delete close cleanup worktree retire
commandbar_cmd_decommission_confirm_aria: Confirm decommission of {desig}? Press Enter again to decommission.
feat_decommission_command_body: ⌘K now carries Decommission for the session you have selected. It takes two presses — the first arms the row, the second decommissions — and the usual undo window still applies.
commandbar_cmd_diagnose_epic_kw: epic diagnose diagnosis unrecognized not recognized why sub-issues children parent
feat_command_bar_v2_title: Command bar now runs commands and searches docs
feat_command_bar_v2_body: ⌘K/Ctrl+K does more than jump between sessions now. A Commands group runs verbs — New task, Broadcast, Settings, Usage, Retry, Jump to next needs-you — and a Docs group searches the documentation and opens the page in a new tab. Just start typing what you want to do.
feat_command_bar_repo_filter_title: Filter sessions from the command bar
feat_command_bar_repo_filter_body: Pick a repository in the command bar, then press ⇧↵ (or ⌘/Ctrl+↵) to filter the session list to that repo's active sessions. Plain ↵ still opens its backlog.
feat_multi_repo_filter_title: Filter the herd by several repos at once
feat_multi_repo_filter_body: Hold Shift and click repo pills in the herd's chip rail to combine more than one repo in the filter — the session list, Up Next, and Owed lenses then show work from every selected repo. A plain click still picks just one; Shift+click toggles a repo in or out.
feat_command_bar_search_field_body: The command bar now has a search field in the top bar with a ⌘K / Ctrl K shortcut. Documentation and Learnings live inside it — search the docs and open Learnings straight from the bar.
feat_command_bar_jump_title: Jump to command-bar results with Alt
feat_command_bar_jump_body: Hold Alt with the command bar open to reveal number hints (1–9, then 0) on the first ten results, then press the digit to jump straight to it.
feat_scroll_to_session_start_body: When you're reading older terminal output, the floating scroll control now includes an ↑ button for the start of the session next to the existing ↓ jump back to the latest prompt.
feat_codex_plan_gate_body: Codex tasks can now use the Plan gate from New Task. Turn it on to make Codex write a plan first, send that plan through Shepherd's adversarial review, and start implementation only after the plan is approved.
feat_codex_usage_gauges_body: The usage popover now shows Codex's 5-hour and weekly rate limits as percentage bars with reset times — the same format as Claude, so you can compare both CLIs at a glance. Raw token counts still show below the bars.
topbar_codex_limits_unavailable: Codex limits not found; showing token usage.
feat_hands_off_epics_body: The Epic panel now walks you through the automation settings that let an epic drain end-to-end on its own. Apply the recommended hands-off defaults in one click, and see what will still legitimately stop it.
epic_handsoff_intro: Recommended automation so this epic drains without operator intervention:
epic_handsoff_item_autopilot: Autopilot — drive each session through stops toward a PR
epic_handsoff_item_automerge: Full-auto merge — land ready PRs automatically (Draft off)
epic_handsoff_item_critic: Critic — auto code-review when CI goes green
epic_handsoff_item_autoaddress: Auto-Address — feed critic findings back to the agent
epic_handsoff_item_plangate: Plan gate — adversarial plan review (keep on)
epic_handsoff_item_epicmode: Epic mode: auto — drain without approving each spawn
epic_handsoff_plangate_note: Plan gate is hands-off-safe: it auto-approves good plans and iterates findings with the agent, only pausing for you if a plan can't be approved in 5 rounds.
epic_handsoff_stops: Still stops for you: a stuck session, a REWORK the agent can't auto-resolve, a plan that can't pass review, a critic error, the usage/credit ceiling, or a diverged child PR.
epic_handsoff_repowide: Apply writes repo-wide automation defaults — not just this epic.
epic_handsoff_mode_failed: Applied the defaults, but couldn't switch the epic to auto mode — set Epic mode in the controls below.
feat_transcription_origin_body: While you dictate, the compose bar now shows a small label naming the source — “Local Whisper” when the voice-whisper plugin handled the recording, “Browser / device” when your browser's speech engine did. It names the origin of that recording only; it is not a plugin health check.
feat_voice_whisper_body: Install the optional voice-whisper plugin and the compose-bar mic transcribes on your own machine via whisper.cpp — consistent quality, nothing leaves the host, and finally a working mic in the iOS home-screen PWA.
feat_live_dictation_body: The local-Whisper compose mic now shows your words live as you dictate — a running preview that updates every couple of seconds, replaced by the precise transcript when you stop. It works even in the iOS home-screen PWA, and stays entirely on your machine.
pluginupdate_intro: Update status of your installed plugins. Plugins with a newer version can be updated in place — Shepherd fetches the new release and loads it.
pluginupdate_manual_hint: A plugin without an update source, or one linked from a local checkout, must be updated at its source and picked up on restart.
settings_plugin_update_count: {count} of your plugins have a newer version
feat_plugin_updates_title: See when your plugins have updates
feat_plugin_updates_body: Settings now checks whether your installed plugins have a newer released version — for plugins that declare a repository or run from a git checkout — and lets you apply the update in place with one click: Shepherd fetches the new release and loads it, no manual git or folder-swapping.
feat_plugin_tab_updates_title: Plugin updates, right on the Plugins tab
feat_plugin_tab_updates_body: The Settings → Plugins tab now shows an "update available" badge with an Update button directly on each plugin's card, and a "Check for updates" button re-scans on demand — no more waiting for the half-hourly background check after you publish a release.
feat_epic_backlog_visibility_title: Epics stand out in the backlog
feat_epic_backlog_visibility_body: In the repo backlog's Issues list, epics now sort to the top and carry an amber accent so they're easy to spot. Expanding one shows its controls (Pause, Attended, Stop epic, Approve next) as proper buttons instead of plain text.
feat_ci_running_banner_body: While CI runs on a session's PR, the terminal now shows a spinning-gear banner — the same spot as the review-in-flight notice — naming the checks in flight, with the PR number linking to the PR on GitHub. Status and automation info always lands in one consistent place, so you know to hold off while CI is still working.
feat_worktree_files_title: Browse the worktree from the Files tab
feat_worktree_files_body: The Files tab now has a Scratchpad / Worktree switch — flip to Worktree to browse and download any file from the session's working tree (read-only, .git hidden). Scratchpad stays the default and still takes uploads.
pluginupdate_applied_restart: Updated to v{version}. Restart Shepherd to load the new code.
pluginupdate_apply_err_symlinked: This plugin runs from a linked source checkout — update it there.
pluginupdate_apply_err_incompatible: The new version needs a newer Shepherd — update Shepherd first.
pluginupdate_apply_err_nosource: No update source — Shepherd can't fetch a new version automatically.
feat_auth_url_banner_title: One-click open for MCP sign-in links
feat_auth_url_banner_body: When an agent needs you to authorize an MCP connection (like Notion), the long sign-in URL used to wrap across terminal lines so you couldn't click or copy it. Now a banner appears above the terminal with the full link and one-click Open and Copy buttons while the agent waits.
feat_clipboard_osc52_body: When Claude copies text in the terminal (`c to copy`), it now lands in your system clipboard. If the browser blocks the automatic write, a small Copy pill appears over the terminal for a one-click copy.
restart_settings_hint: Restart the Shepherd service in place. Running agent sessions are not affected — they live in herdr, not in Shepherd.
restart_confirm_body: Shepherd restarts and this page reloads once it is back — usually within a few seconds. Running agent sessions are not affected.
restart_herdr_note: The daemon hands its panes to a fresh server (live-handoff). Agent sessions survive; attached terminal clients reconnect briefly.
restart_in_progress: Restarting Shepherd — waiting for it to come back…
restart_timeout: Shepherd did not come back in time. Check the service, or restart it manually:
restart_err_not_systemd: This Shepherd instance is not managed by systemd (a dev instance?), so it cannot restart itself. Restart it manually:
restart_err_already: A restart is already in progress.
restart_err_generic: Could not start the restart. Restart manually if the problem persists:
feat_restart_shepherd_body: Settings → Session now has a Restart Shepherd action, and the plugin restart banners gained a Restart-now button — no more copy-pasting the systemctl command. Optionally the herdr daemon restarts too, gracefully: live panes are handed off and survive.
feat_newtask_prompt_mic_body: The New Task prompt field now has a mic — same engines as the compose bar: the browser's dictation, or local Whisper via the voice-whisper plugin (the only mic in an iOS home-screen PWA). While you speak, a live preview renders in the field; on stop the accurate full-clip transcript replaces it.
feat_plan_reviewer_environment_title: See which CLI reviewed a plan
feat_plan_reviewer_environment_body: Plan Gate headings now show the task session and reviewer coding CLI, model and effort. Open the info popover beside the chips for the exact Settings path and documentation link.
planpanel_env_help_aria: Where to change plan and review coding environments
planpanel_env_popover_body: Plan shows the task session's coding CLI, model and reasoning effort. Review shows the Plan Gate reviewer that checked this plan. Change task defaults in New Task, repo defaults or global defaults; change the reviewer under Settings -> CLIs -> Planner (plan reviewer).
feat_usage_gauge_provider_rotation_body: When Claude Code and Codex usage data are both configured, the compact top-bar gauge now alternates every two minutes between CC and CX. Codex shows rate-limit bars when available, or token totals until the CLI reports reset windows.
feat_codex_bring_back_body: Bring back now works for Codex sessions too, not just Claude. Select a done Codex session in the Done lens and click Bring back — Shepherd re-creates its worktree and resumes the exact Codex conversation by its session id. Only isolated Codex sessions can be brought back; others still need a relaunch.
newtask_drop_hint_keyboard: or drop files here, or paste an image with {shortcut}
feat_session_tooltip_body: See a task's full launch details — prompt, issue, attached file names, branch, launch checkboxes, coding CLI, model, effort, and sandbox state. On desktop, hover or keyboard-focus the task id above the terminal; on touch, tap and hold it. A single tap now only folds the header or toggles the git rail.
feat_provider_capacity_gauge_title: Compare Coding CLI capacity before you pick
feat_provider_capacity_gauge_body: The New Task form now shows Claude Code and Codex side by side with their remaining usage room below the Coding CLI selector, so you can choose the provider with the most headroom before you start a run.
feat_provider_capacity_reset_times_title: Capacity now shows when it resets
feat_provider_capacity_reset_times_body: The New Task form now adds the reset time to each Claude Code and Codex capacity row, so 80% free also tells you whether that room lasts until tonight or later in the week.
planpanel_quota_unreachable: Couldn't reach the planning pane. The plan was not resumed.
planpanel_quota_not_stalled: This plan is no longer stalled. Refresh or review again if the state looks stale.
planpanel_quota_failed: Couldn't update the stalled plan. Please try again.
epic_start_title: Click to start this epic. Shepherd launches the next eligible child issue and continues according to the selected mode.
epic_pause_title: Click to pause this epic runner. Existing sessions and progress stay intact.
epic_mode_auto_title: Click to switch to attended mode. Shepherd will wait for you to approve each next child issue.
epic_mode_auto_aria: Auto: switch epic to attended mode
epic_mode_attended_title: Click to switch to auto mode. Shepherd will launch eligible child issues without asking for each approval.
epic_mode_attended_aria: Attended: switch epic to auto mode
automation_preview_open_mode_hint: Controls what happens when you click a live Preview chip for this repo.
feat_preview_open_mode_body: Each repo can now open Preview chips inline, in a new browser tab, or ask each time.
issues_filter_no_match: no issues match the active filters
feat_issue_author_label_filter_title: Filter issues by author and label
feat_issue_author_label_filter_body: Issue lists now show each issue's author, and the Filters menu can narrow the backlog by author and by label — pick an author or toggle one or more labels to focus the list.
feat_review_live_preview_body: While a PR-critic or plan-gate review runs off-screen, the amber banner now shows a live tail of what the reviewer is doing — its last few actions — and the terminal dims to signal that Shepherd is working and there's no need to type. The dim is visual only; your prompt stays usable.
gitrail_review_plan_approved: Plan already approved — nothing to re-review.
hold_reviewing_findings: Re-reviewing the plan ({count} findings from the last round).
hold_cta_go_title: Approve the plan and start execution
hold_cta_rereview_title: Send the plan back for another review
hold_cta_answer_title: Open the plan to answer its open questions
hold_cta_answer_reply_title: Open the session to answer and continue
feat_hold_cta_title: Act on parked sessions from the row
feat_hold_cta_body: A parked plan-gate session now explains why it's waiting right on its row — awaiting re-review, plan approved, or review stuck — and offers the matching one-click action (Re-review, Go, Resume, or Answer) without opening the session.
hold_cta_resume_failed: Couldn't resume the plan review. Try again.
hold_cta_retry_ci_title: Re-run the PR's failed CI jobs
hold_cta_retry_ci_unsupported: Retrying CI isn't available for this repo's forge.
hold_cta_retry_ci_no_run: No failed CI run found to retry.
feat_retry_ci_cta_title: Retry failed CI from the row
feat_retry_ci_cta_body: When a session's PR has failing CI, its row now offers a one-click Retry CI action — it re-runs the PR head's latest failed workflow (just the broken jobs) without opening the session or GitHub.
settings_operator_language_hint: The language Shepherd's agents use to talk to you — status, questions, plans, handoffs. Independent of this interface's language. Code, commands, logs, and GitHub text stay in their original language.
feat_operator_language_body: Set an operator language in Settings and Shepherd's spawned agents write their status updates, questions, plan explanations, and handoffs in that language. Code, commands, logs, commit messages, and GitHub issue/PR text stay in their original language.
feat_attention_highlight_body: When an agent stops and needs your call (a menu choice, a plan-review decision, a halt on an error), its card in the herd now carries a faint red wash, so you can spot who is waiting at a glance.
planpanel_no_verdict: The plan reviewer did not produce a verdict.
recap_skip_metadata_mismatch_body: The session row points at branch `{branch}`, but the archived worktree was on `{current}`. Shepherd did not trust the empty diff.
recap_skip_base_refresh_failed_body: Shepherd found landed-work evidence ({evidence}), but refreshing the base ref failed, so the empty diff could not be trusted.
recap_skip_ancestry_check_failed_body: Shepherd found landed-work evidence ({evidence}), but could not verify whether HEAD is already contained in `{baseRef}`.
recap_skip_empty_diff_contradicted_headl: Recap skipped: empty diff contradicted landed-work evidence
recap_skip_empty_diff_contradicted_body: Shepherd found landed-work evidence ({evidence}), but HEAD was not contained in `{baseRef}` even though the diff was empty.
recap_skip_evidence_review: PR review recorded for this head
feat_blocked_issues_title: Blocked issues stay out of your way
feat_blocked_issues_body: Up Next no longer suggests issues that are blocked by an open dependency, and the backlog now flags them with a "blocked on #N" badge so you can see what they're waiting on.
epic_diag_open_title: Analyze this epic's structure and recommend repairs
epic_diag_clean: No structural problems found — this epic looks well-formed.
epic_diag_import_hint: Wires each member as a native GitHub sub-issue and each dependency as a blocked-by link. This edits the GitHub issue.
epic_diag_no_children_body: Shepherd sees no children for this epic. Add native GitHub sub-issues, or reference real child issue numbers in the parent body via an epic-dag fence or a - [ ] #123 checklist.
epic_diag_markdown_source_body: Shepherd is reading this epic from the parent body rather than native GitHub links. Native sub-issues are safer for gating, especially in large repos — use Import structure to convert.
epic_diag_truncated_body: This markdown epic resolves children against the repo's open-issue list, read capped at 200. A child beyond the cap can be misread as closed and spawned early. Import into native links to make gating exact.
epic_diag_all_parallel_body: {count} children are ready with no dependency edges, so Shepherd starts them all at once. If some must land first, add blocked-by links or #child <- #blocker lines.
epic_diag_self_dep_body: Child #{child} lists itself as a blocker. Shepherd ignores that edge — remove it.
epic_diag_outside_dep_body: Child #{child} is blocked by #{blocker}, which isn't a member of this epic. Shepherd ignores that edge — add #{blocker} to the epic or remove the dependency.
epic_diag_native_body_disagree_body: Shepherd is using the native sub-issues. The parent body's list differs — only in body: {onlyInBody}; only in native: {onlyInNative}. Reconcile the body or re-import so they match.
epic_diag_entry_hint: Enter an existing parent issue in the selected repo — the would-be [[epic|epic]] you expected Shepherd to recognize. A number that isn't a real issue reports the same "not recognized" result.
feat_epic_diagnosis_body: A new Diagnose button on each epic panel analyzes the epic's GitHub structure and recommends concrete repairs — the recognized source, ordering risks, and how to fix a malformed epic. Markdown epics can import into native links right from the diagnosis. Can't find a Diagnose button because Shepherd shows nothing for a would-be epic? Open the command bar (⌘K/Ctrl+K) and run "Diagnose epic…" to diagnose any parent by number.
epicdraft_empty: No draft yet — the shaping session is still gathering context and questions.
epicdraft_awaiting_hint: Review the proposed epic. Approve to create the issues on GitHub, amend to ask the session for changes, or abort to discard.
epicdraft_amend_placeholder: Ask the session for a change…
newtask_epic_authoring_hint: Guided shaping of a rough idea into a reviewable EPIC draft (parent, child issues, dependency DAG). No code; nothing is written to GitHub until you approve the draft.
feat_epic_research_flow_body: Turn a rough product idea into a structured epic. Check "Create EPIC from research" in New Task and an attended session shapes the work, then proposes a reviewable draft — parent issue, child issues and their dependency order. Nothing is written to GitHub until you approve; then Shepherd creates the issues and links, ready to drain.
feat_prewarm_epic_ci_body: When enabled, an epic's landing PR now opens early as a draft so its CI runs warm while the epic still drains, instead of a cold first run at completion. Opt in per repo in Automation settings.
feat_colored_issue_labels_title: Issue labels in their real colors
feat_colored_issue_labels_body: Label chips across the backlog, filters, and prompt sources now render each label's actual color from GitHub, normalized for legibility in both light and dark themes — instead of flat grey.
issues_filter_blocked_title: Hide issues with a blocked label (e.g. blocked-upstream)
issues_filter_all_blocked: All matching issues are blocked — toggle off to see them
feat_hide_blocked_toggle_body: A "Hide blocked issues" toggle now works the same way across the backlog and the New Task issue picker — on by default, it drops any issue labeled as blocked. (Up Next always shows only startable work, so blocked issues never appear there.)
feat_issue_context_menu_title: Right-click an issue for quick actions
feat_issue_context_menu_body: Right-click (or long-press) an issue in the New Task dialog or the backlog to open it on the forge, preview its details, or drop one of your issue steers straight into the composer — pre-filled and ready to review, not launched.
feat_diff_review_body: The session Diff tab now renders through a new engine: switch between side-by-side and unified views, with word-level intra-line highlighting, a file sidebar for quick navigation, and collapsible unchanged context.
feat_repo_chip_github_link_title: Open GitHub from a repo chip
feat_repo_chip_github_link_body: Right-click or hold a GitHub-backed repo chip and open the repository page directly from the herd header.
heartbeat_pop_intro: The agent's tool-use over the last 8 minutes (each cell ≈ 20 s). An empty strip means the session has gone quiet.
heartbeat_legend_active_desc: Tool-use in that ~20 s slice — brighter means busier.
heartbeat_legend_error_desc: A tool-use in that slice failed.
feat_heartbeat_legend_title: Activity heartbeat explains itself on hover
feat_heartbeat_legend_body: The amber activity strip on live session cards now opens a legend when you hover it on desktop — so you can see what it tracks: the agent's tool-use over the last 8 minutes (brighter = busier, red = an error, empty = quiet).
issuerow_epic_others_notice: Already in progress by {who} — you can still start
issuerow_epic_owner_notice: Set up by {who} — you can still start
feat_epic_in_flight_title: See who's already on an epic
feat_epic_in_flight_body: The backlog now flags epics someone else is already working on — a pill shows how many children are in progress and by whom, plus a soft notice near Start — so you don't start an epic a teammate already owns.
feat_assigned_others_plain_body: When you switch the backlog's “mine & unassigned” filter off, plain issues assigned to someone else now show an “assigned to X” pill — and a soft notice reminds you that you can still start one. It only appears once you toggle that filter off.
issuerow_epic_assigned_notice: Assigned to {who} — you can still start
newtask_base_missing: Repo has no commit yet. Create the initial commit, then run the task.
feat_initial_commit_repair_title: Repair empty repos from New Task
feat_initial_commit_repair_body: When a selected repo has no initial commit yet, New Task now blocks the doomed launch and offers to create the first empty commit in place so the task can start from a real base.
newtask_provider_constraint_error: {token} requires {provider}. Remove it from the prompt or switch engines.
feat_provider_aware_skills_body: The New Task and compose pickers now understand Claude slash commands and direct Codex skills. Use / for Claude, $ for Codex, or bare @ as a Codex picker alias; rows show whether they run in Claude, Codex, or both.
herd_help_active: Shepherd is running the coding agent autonomously in its own worktree — no babysitting. When it finishes it opens a pull request and moves down this list.
herd_help_ci_running: The agent opened a pull request and its CI checks are running. Green hands it off for review or your merge; a failure drops it into CI failed.
herd_help_ci_failed: A pull request's CI checks failed — the work is done but needs a look. It's back in your court: open it, or send the agent to fix it.
herd_help_reviewing: Shepherd's automated reviewer, the Critic, is checking this diff for bugs and quality. If it finds problems the agent reworks them automatically; if it's clean, the PR is handed off.
herd_help_rework: The agent is automatically addressing the changes the Plan gate or Critic asked for — no manual round-trip. When it's done it re-runs CI and review.
herd_help_waiting_reviewer: The pull request is green and handed to the named reviewer — their turn, not yours. Shepherd surfaces whose turn it is so nothing stalls silently.
herd_help_waiting_merger: The pull request is approved and green, waiting on the named person to merge it. Shepherd tracks it so it doesn't fall through the cracks.
herd_help_draft_signoff: A draft pull request with green CI, parked for your sign-off before it can merge. Review it, then mark it ready when you're happy.
herd_help_your_turn: A pull request is open, CI is green, and the agent handed off — your turn to review and merge. This is the payoff: finished work waiting for a click.
herd_help_ready: You've flagged these pull requests as ready to land. Run the Merge train to merge them in one go; Shepherd handles the merge and cleans up the worktree.
herd_help_merging: The Merge train is landing these pull requests for you, one after another — no manual merging. As each lands it moves to Merged.
herd_help_merged: These pull requests have landed on your main branch — done. Shepherd tears down their worktrees; any leftover manual steps show up on the Owed lens.
feat_herd_stage_help_body: Every stage on the Herd now has a small "i" that explains what it means, what Shepherd is doing for you, and what happens next — from Working through Reviewing to Merged. Hover it (or tap on mobile). New here? The empty board also shows how work flows end to end.
feat_diff_annotations_title: Agent notes in the Diff tab
feat_diff_annotations_body: The Diff tab now shows the agent's own reasoning inline, anchored to the lines its edits produced, plus any code-review findings as banners on the files they concern — so the why behind a change lives next to the change.
feat_plan_reviewer_env_body: When a Plan Gate review is running, the Reviewing button now shows which coding CLI, model, and effort are doing the review — so you know exactly who's checking your plan before execution.
unitrow_merge_blocked_title: Branch protection is blocking merge after checks cleared
feat_review_block_cues_title: Review blocks are visible on session cards
feat_review_block_cues_body: When the configured human reviewer requests changes, the session card and herd groups now show Changes requested instead of quietly waiting on that reviewer.
feat_codex_discovery_body: The prompt picker now understands Codex skills, including installed plugin skills such as $github:yeet, while installed Codex plugins appear as a browsing-only inventory.
files_source_difference_tip: Scratchpad contains supplementary files for this session and stays separate from the repository. Worktree is the agent's project directory and changes; you can only browse and download it here.
newtask_provider_constraint_body: Shepherd switched to {provider} and disabled incompatible engines while {token} is in the prompt.
feat_provider_availability_card_body: New Task now shows a blue CLI-availability card when a picked command, skill, or plugin token only runs in one coding CLI, so the forced provider choice is visible before launch.
feat_newtask_image_preview_title: Preview images before starting a task
feat_newtask_image_preview_body: Hover or focus an image attachment's filename to check it before launch. On mobile, tap the filename to toggle the preview.
feat_automerge_session_jump_body: Click any active Full-auto merge entry to jump straight to its task and terminal. Shepherd clears filters that would hide it and follows the task into the right repository.
feat_build_queue_progress_toggle_title: Open the build queue from progress
feat_build_queue_progress_toggle_body: Click a session's progress badge to select it and open its build queue above the terminal. Click the selected session's badge again to collapse or expand the queue.
settings_default_codex_model_hint: Sets the Codex model for new tasks and Codex-backed autonomous starts. Default lets the local Codex CLI choose without a --model flag.
settings_default_codex_model_save_failed: Couldn't change the default Codex model. Retry.
settings_default_environment_hint: Choose the CLI and its model for new tasks and unattended work. Shepherd remembers a separate model for Claude Code and Codex.
feat_default_coding_environment_title: Choose a default CLI and model
feat_default_coding_environment_body: Settings now remembers separate default models for Claude Code and Codex. New tasks and unattended starts use the model paired with the selected coding CLI.
feat_mobile_herd_accordion_body: The phone herd now keeps only one named lifecycle group open at a time. Your turn opens by default; tap any larger group header to switch or close it while active sessions stay visible.
feat_review_job_model_body: The terminal review banner now shows the coding CLI, model and effort used by both Plan Gate and PR critic jobs, so their environment is distinct from the task session.
epicdraft_approve_in_progress: Still creating the epic — this is taking a while. It'll appear here when it lands.
plangate_review_at_cap: Plan review started, but the rework budget is spent: if it requests changes, those findings won't be sent to the agent.
plangate_review_spends_round: Re-reviews send the findings back to the agent and spend a rework round ({round}/{cap} used).
planpanel_review_at_cap: Re-reviewing at the rework cap. The reviewer can still approve the plan, but if it requests changes again those findings will not be sent to the planning agent. Use “Resume with findings” to reset the budget and re-send them.
planpanel_review_at_cap_no_resume: Re-reviewing at the rework cap. The reviewer can still approve the plan. If it requests changes instead, those findings will not be sent to the planning agent — “Resume with findings” then appears here to reset the budget and re-send them.
epic_hold_epic_base_unavailable: Paused: the epic branch {branch} could not be created on the forge. Retrying — no child can be started until it exists.
settings_role_model_distiller_hint: Finds recurring lessons from repository signals.
settings_distiller_interval_hint: Choose {min}–{max} whole days. Manual runs are never delayed.
settings_distiller_interval_save_failed: Could not save the Distiller interval.
feat_distiller_provider_cadence_body: Choose the Distiller CLI, model, effort, and the days between automatic runs.
feat_collapsible_coding_cli_sections_bod: Coding CLI settings are now grouped into collapsible sections in a practical order: global defaults, Claude Code, Codex, then helper-agent environments.
feat_consistent_issue_selectors_body: New Task, Repos, and Up Next now share the same compact issue layout, forge-label colors, and responsive hierarchy while keeping their surface-specific actions.
settings_role_model_rundown_hint: Summarizes herd-wide attention once per day and on demand.
feat_rundown_provider_body: Choose the Herd Rundown CLI, model, and effort for automatic and regenerated digests.
settings_role_model_optimizer_hint: Strengthens rules marked as not working using their failure evidence.
feat_optimizer_provider_aware_body: Choose the CLI, model, and reasoning effort the Optimizer uses when strengthening rules marked as not working.
settings_role_model_merge_suggest_hint: Consolidates overlapping Learnings rules within and across repositories.
feat_merge_suggester_provider_body: Choose the Learnings Merge Suggester CLI, model, and effort.
updatemodal_dirty_title: Local changes are blocking the update
updatemodal_dirty_body: You have uncommitted changes in the repo where Shepherd runs, so it can't pull the new version cleanly. Discard your changes to update, or leave them and update later after committing them yourself.
updatemodal_discard_confirm: Reset {count} tracked change(s) to the last commit? Edits to existing files are lost; newly added files aren't deleted — they stay as untracked files.
updatemodal_now_clean: The tree is clean now — try the update again.
updatemodal_dirty_stale: The changes shifted since they were shown — review the updated list and confirm again.
updatemodal_dirty_too_large: The changes are too large to verify safely for a one-click discard — please commit or discard them manually.
feat_safe_update_dirty_repo_body: When an update can't run because you have uncommitted changes in the repo where Shepherd runs, the update dialog now explains it in plain language and lists the changed files. You can discard exactly those changes and update in one click, or leave them and update later — newly added files are always kept.
recap_no_diff_warning: No files were changed in this session, so there is no code diff.
recap_empty_legacy: No recap was created because this session had no file changes. Older Shepherd versions skipped these sessions because there was no diff.
recap_failure_source_headline: The session sources needed for the recap were unavailable.
recap_failure_launch_headline: The recap agent could not be started.
recap_failure_no_result_headline: The recap agent returned no result.
recap_failure_invalid_result_headline: The recap agent returned an unusable result.
recap_failure_auth_action: Configure authentication for the recap provider before relaunching the session.
recap_failure_source_action: Keep the repository and worktree available until completion, then relaunch the session if a recap is still needed.
recap_failure_provider_action: Check the recap provider and model before relaunching the session.
feat_recap_diagnostics_body: Finished sessions now show how they were completed and why a recap is missing. No-change sessions still get a recap with a clear no-diff warning; technical failures include actionable guidance and redacted details.
feat_desktop_herd_group_collapse_title: Fold lifecycle groups on the desktop
feat_desktop_herd_group_collapse_body: The desktop session rail's lifecycle group headers (CI running, Your turn, Merged, …) are now collapsible too: click a header to fold that group away — each group toggles independently, and jumps to a session hidden in a folded group reopen it automatically.
feat_resizable_repos_body: On desktop the Repos window now opens much larger and remembers your size: drag the bottom-right corner to resize the whole window, and drag the divider between the repository list and the detail pane to widen either side. Both sizes are saved in this browser; double-click a handle to reset.
feat_scratchpad_attachments_title: Task attachments show in the Files tab
feat_scratchpad_attachments_body: Screenshots and files you attach when creating a task now appear in the session's Files tab from the start, under a new Attachments folder in the Scratchpad view — alongside any images you paste in mid-session. It works for every agent, including ones without a scratchpad of their own.
diagnostics_hint_host_capacity_herdr_unb: Shepherd's own service is bounded, but the herdr service that runs your agent sessions has no memory or CPU ceiling — a runaway session could still starve the host. Add a [[host-capacity|host capacity]] limit to herdr too.
diagnostics_fix_action_host_capacity: Shepherd will set MemoryHigh={memoryHigh} and CPUQuota={cpuQuota} on these units: {units}. This applies a live, persistent limit — no restart, no interrupted sessions. It bounds each unit on its own; for one shared cap across Shepherd and herdr, set up a shepherd.slice (see How to fix).
feat_host_capacity_fix_body: When Settings → Diagnostics warns that Shepherd — or the herdr service that runs your agents — has no memory or CPU ceiling, you can now apply a conservative, host-derived limit in one click (shown for your review first). It's a live, persistent systemd limit with no restart. Available on user-scoped installs; the guardrail check now covers herdr too.
diagnostics_fix_unresolved_host_capacity: Shepherd applied the limits, but the check is still not OK — give it a moment, or check the unit is user-scoped.
hold_pr_conflict: The PR has merge conflicts — CI can't run until it's rebased.
statusbar_aria: Session status; the agent model and effort shown are the configured values, which the runtime may substitute at spawn
statusbar_tokens_unavailable_title: Token usage is unavailable for this session
statusbar_tokens_unavailable_codex_title: Token usage isn't tracked for Codex sessions yet
statusbar_elapsed_live_title: Session age {elapsed} — time since creation, not active runtime
statusbar_elapsed_done_title: Session age {elapsed} — creation to archive, not active runtime
feat_session_status_bar_title: One status bar for every session
feat_session_status_bar_body: Every session view now ends in the same status strip: the CLI, model and effort the agent is configured with, its token usage, and its session age. It stays put on every tab and lifecycle stage — including rework, where the banner above shows the reviewer while the strip keeps the task agent's own identity — and on finished sessions in the Done lens.
statusbar_identity_title: Configured environment: {identity}. The runtime may substitute at spawn time (usage downgrade, provider clamps) without changing this configuration.
newtask_readiness_empty_prompt: type a prompt or pick an issue to start
newtask_readiness_base_missing: base branch missing — repair it above
newtask_alpha_caution: Alpha: runs unattended, sandbox forced to trusted.
promptsources_more_row: ↓ {count} more — type # in the prompt to search
gloss_plan_gate_def: A pre-execution checkpoint: the agent first researches the task and writes an implementation plan, which is adversarially reviewed and must be approved before any product code is written.
gloss_autopilot_def: Best-effort automation that drives a task autonomously through to an open pull request. Switched off, each step stays with the operator to discuss, iterate and approve; the per-task toggle overrides the repo's standing Autopilot default.
feat_new_task_redesign_body: The New Task dialog is now a single view: the prompt front and center with an in-field toolbar, run settings grouped in a side rail, and the Create & Run button always visible. Type # in the prompt to search open issues inline.
feat_gear_menu_redesign_title: The gear menu is now a telemetry panel
feat_gear_menu_redesign_body: The gear now always opens a redesigned menu: a live token-usage gauge (tap “all” for the per-window breakdown), a Halt-herd action with a live working count, grouped workspace, plugin and support rows, and the app version + connection state up top. On desktop, Cmd/Ctrl+, opens Settings from anywhere.
diagnostics_hint_tmp_inodes_ok: The temporary filesystem has plenty of [[inode|inodes]] free.
diagnostics_hint_tmp_inodes_high: The temporary filesystem is running low on [[inode|inodes]] — it can run out while still showing free space, and agent sessions then fail with confusing "no space" errors. Fix sweeps the caches Shepherd can reclaim.
diagnostics_hint_tmp_inodes_critical: The temporary filesystem is nearly out of [[inode|inodes]]. New agent sessions will start failing with "no space" errors even though disk space looks free. Fix sweeps the caches Shepherd can reclaim.
diagnostics_hint_tmp_inodes_uninspectabl: Shepherd can't read [[inode|inode]] usage for the temporary filesystem on this deployment.
diagnostics_fix_action_tmp_inodes: Sweep Shepherd's reclaimable caches from the temporary filesystem now, ignoring the usual usage threshold. This drops the compile cache and stale tool caches. Package-manager stores and leftover worktrees left behind by agents are not reclaimed yet, so usage may stay high.
diagnostics_fix_unresolved_tmp_inodes: Shepherd swept the caches it can reclaim, but usage is still high — the rest is most likely a package-manager store or a leftover worktree an agent left in the temporary filesystem, which Shepherd doesn't reclaim yet.
gloss_inode_def: A filesystem's record for one file or directory. A filesystem has a limited number of them, set when it is created — so it can run out of inodes while still having free space, and every new file then fails as though the disk were full.
feat_herdr_sandbox_advisory_title: Sandboxed sessions on herdr 0.7.5: a status caveat
feat_herdr_sandbox_advisory_body: On herdr 0.7.5, a resting sandboxed agent shows as "working" until it asks a question or finishes — herdr can't report its idle state (issue #1716). Trusted sessions are unaffected. If you rely on sandboxed sessions and want accurate idle status, the herdr update dialog now offers a one-click downgrade to a version without the regression.
feat_herdr_downgrade_title: Stranded on a broken herdr? One-click downgrade
feat_herdr_downgrade_body: If herdr auto-updated into a version Shepherd cannot drive (0.7.5+ broke agent spawning), Shepherd now heals itself: the herdr-update dialog and the Diagnose tab offer a one-click downgrade to the highest supported version — downloaded, verified, swapped and restarted entirely in-app, with a step-by-step audit log.
codexupdate_notes_incomplete: The full release history is not available here yet. You can still update now or view the source on GitHub.
feat_codex_release_notes_body: The Codex update dialog now shows the original GitHub release notes for every npm-published stable version in your update range, newest first.
